jobs.title: Director, Product Management - Corporate Solutions
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Director, Product Management - Corporate Solutions The Product & Commercialization Strategy, Corporate Solutions, will be responsible for identifying, developing, and delivering issuer- and client-centric product solutions across the Middle East & Africa (MEA), aligned to both regional and global priorities. This role will drive the growth and differentiation of Mastercard's Commercial Solutions portfolio by bringing new products, partnerships, and value-added services to market, while developing commercialization strategies that accelerate adoption and revenue growth. The successful candidate will operate at the intersection of product, sales, partnerships, and strategy, translating market opportunities into scalable solutions and commercial outcomes across Business, Corporate, and Government segments. The role is responsible for supporting the development and execution of initiatives that generate incremental sales volume, revenue growth, and competitive differentiation for Mastercard and its customers, while serving as a key link between regional market needs and global product priorities. Key Responsibilities 1. Product Strategy & Innovation •Lead the regional rollout and adoption of global Corporate Solutions products, acting as the primary feedback bridge between regional markets and global product teams. •Identify, evaluate, and launch new products, partnerships, and value-added services across the Corporate Solutions portfolio, including co-brands, rewards, and differentiated customer value propositions. •Develop business cases, market assessments, and go-to-market strategies to support product expansion and innovation priorities. 2. Commercialization & Sales Enablement •Develop commercialization strategies, sales plays, and value propositions that enable Business Development teams to accelerate growth across key customer segments and use cases. •Identify and scale best practices from global and regional markets, translating them into repeatable and actionable growth opportunities. •Partner closely with sales teams on strategic opportunities, customer engagements, proposal responses, and solution positioning while monitoring market trends and competitive activity. 3. Ecosystem Development & Strategic Growth •Identify and develop growth opportunities across the broader B2B payments ecosystem, including fintechs, ERP providers, expense management platforms, and strategic partners. •Drive strategic initiatives and partnership opportunities that expand market reach, solution capabilities, and revenue growth. •Serve as a thought partner to regional leadership on business growth, market expansion, ecosystem strategy, and innovation priorities. Preferred Experience •10+ years of experience across product management, strategy consulting, commercialization, business development, or payments. •Strong understanding of commercial payments, corporate cards, treasury, expense management, accounts payable, and B2B payment ecosystems. •Demonstrated experience launching products, developing go-to-market strategies, and driving cross-functional initiatives. •Strong commercial acumen with the ability to translate market opportunities into scalable business outcomes. •Exceptional stakeholder management, executive communication, and influencing skills. •Experience working across global, regional, and market-level teams in a highly matrixed environment. jobs.description: Do you love being part of a successful team? Do you relish opportunities to create improvements and promote operational efficiencies? Join our team Industry Energy technology part of Baker Hughes Company operates across 120 countries and serves the entire energy value-chain and industrial sector. We hold an extensive portfolio of rotating equipment, process flow, transmission technology and lifecycle services. Our mechanical Engineering Team uses cutting-edge technology to design gas and steam turbines, compressors and expanders. Partner with the best As a Test Engineer, you will be conducting product Testing during Pre-FAT and FAT schedule. Confirming the functionality of the design and products in alignment to the customers approved designs. Identifying tools required for testing and ensure sufficient testing capacity exists to achieve all customer commitments. Ensuring all customer commitments are met in a timely, quality-focused, & compliant manner. Identifying opportunities to drive process and operations excellence via standardization, simplification, and automation. To be successful in this role, you will: Have a bachelor's degree from an accredited university or college (or a high school diploma / GED) Have at least 1-2 years of experience in instrumentation, electrical or mechanical engineering. Have strong oral and written communication skills. Be flexible to work 5 days / week on site. Be able to work independently. Have strong problem-solving skills. Be able to work in the UAE without company sponsorship or legal restriction. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. jobs.title: Assistant Resident Engineer
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: We are seeking experienced **Marine Engineers - ARE** to join our dynamic engineering team in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will take on significant portions of marine engineering projects, applying your technical expertise and analytical mindset to design, develop, and optimize marine systems and vessel components. You will work collaboratively with senior engineers and cross-functional teams to deliver innovative solutions that meet rigorous maritime standards and project objectives. This position offers the opportunity to grow your professional expertise while contributing to meaningful marine engineering initiatives in a fast-paced, supportive environment. Perform detailed engineering analysis and calculations for marine systems, propulsion systems, and structural components using established procedures and industry best practices Gather, correlate, and analyze marine engineering data from various sources to support design and implementation decisions Develop and review technical drawings and specifications using CAD software and marine design tools Conduct routine and detailed engineering assessments involving marine systems testing and performance evaluation Propose innovative approaches to solve complex marine engineering problems by modifying standard procedures based on previous project experience Identify discrepancies in engineering results and recommend corrective actions with transparency and precision Provide technical guidance and mentorship to junior engineers and entry-level team members Manage assigned engineering tasks within agreed-upon budgets and schedules with minimal supervision Independently complete primary design elements and technical documentation for marine engineering works Ensure all work complies with maritime regulations, international standards (IMO, ABS, DNV), and organizational quality requirements Collaborate with project managers, clients, and stakeholders to ensure project goals are met efficiently and effectively **Minimum Qualifications:** Bachelor's degree in Marine Engineering, Naval Architecture, Mechanical Engineering, or a related engineering discipline 4+ years of professional experience in marine engineering, vessel design, or related maritime projects Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with demonstrated ability to work through complex technical challenges Proficiency in CAD software and marine design tools Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within team environments Valid professional identification and eligibility to work in the United Arab Emirates **Preferred Qualifications:** Master's degree in Marine Engineering, Naval Architecture, Ocean Engineering, or a related field Professional Engineer (PE) registration or equivalent professional certification in marine engineering Advanced knowledge of marine propulsion systems, hydrodynamics, and vessel performance Experience with maritime regulatory compliance and international marine standards (IMO, ABS, DNV, ClassNK) Proficiency in GIS and specialized marine engineering software Project management experience on marine or offshore engineering projects Experience with beneficial use of marine resources and sustainable marine engineering practices Demonstrated experience mentoring and developing junior engineers Willingness to travel for project site visits and client meetings (typically one to three days) Strong organizational and time management skills with proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

jobs.description: • Works under direct supervision on routine tasks as per established procedures.  • Works with other team members and/or end-users to troubleshoot basic problems.  • Effectively contributes to team goals. • Participates in the development of basic solutions. • Coordinates with Project Managers and IT/Records Management on the proper methods of protection for project records and makes recommendations on media, rotation, procedures, etc. • Understands project needs and determines proper resources for projects. • Evaluates external tools and resources. • Prepares statistical reports on usage and efficiency of projects. • Proactively identifies opportunities for improvement and cost savings. • Analyzes and selects information services. • Answers correspondence on special reference subjects. • Selects and orders new resource materials. • May be designated according to specialized functions.   Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Information Management, or related field 2-4 years of experience in document control or related field Proficiency in document management systems and Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Strong knowledge of database management, filing, and archiving systems Familiarity with digital document management tools Basic understanding of project management principles Knowledge of industry-specific regulations and standards Excellent attention to detail and quality control skills Outstanding organizational and time management abilities Strong communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team Solid problem-solving and analytical skills Adaptability to changing priorities and deadlines

jobs.title: Technician - Digital Delivery
jobs.description: Responsibilities - Develop and implement advanced BIM strategies and workflows for complex construction projects - Coordinate with cross-functional teams to ensure effective BIM integration throughout the project lifecycle - Perform sophisticated 3D modeling, clash detection and coordination using cutting‑edge BIM software - Analyze and optimize building designs for efficiency, sustainability and constructability - Generate detailed reports and visualizations to support data‑driven decision‑making processes - Mentor and train junior BIM specialists and project team members - Collaborate with architects, engineers and contractors to resolve design and construction challenges - Ensure compliance with local building codes and standards in Abu Dhabi and the UAE - Stay at the forefront of BIM technologies and industry best practices, implementing innovative solutions - Lead BIM‑related meetings and presentations with internal and external stakeholders Qualifications - Bachelor's degree in Architecture, Engineering or a related field - BIM certification such as Autodesk Certified Professional - Minimum of 5 years of experience in BIM implementation and management with a focus on large‑scale projects in the Middle East region - Advanced proficiency in BIM software including Autodesk Revit and Navisworks - Expert‑level skills in 3D modeling, visualization and clash detection - Comprehensive knowledge of building information management and data analysis - Proficiency in project management software and reporting tools - In‑depth understanding of the construction industry and building lifecycle processes - Familiarity with UAE building codes, standards and local construction practices in Abu Dhabi - Strong grasp of sustainable design principles and their application in BIM - Excellent analytical and problem‑solving skills with a detail‑oriented approach - Outstanding communication and leadership abilities Benefits We offer comprehensive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees, including medical, dental, vision, life, AD&D, disability benefits, paid time off, leaves of absence, voluntary benefits, perks, flexible work options, wellness resources, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, service recognition awards, retirement savings plan and employee stock purchase plan.

jobs.title: Senior Design Engineer - Building
jobs.description: • Lead and manage the design development of RTA infrastructure projects including roads, interchanges, utilities, drainage, structures, and associated works in accordance with RTA standards and guidelines. • Review, coordinate, and approve design deliverables at various stages including concept, preliminary, detailed, and IFC designs. • Ensure all designs comply with RTA manuals, Dubai Authority requirements (DM, DEWA, Etisalat, Du), and applicable international standards. • Coordinate multidisciplinary design activities including highways, traffic, structures, drainage, utilities, lighting, ITS, and landscaping. • Review and provide technical input on design calculations, drawings, specifications, BOQs, and cost estimates. • Manage design interfaces between roadworks, utilities, structures, and existing infrastructure to eliminate clashes and design conflicts. • Review and respond to RTA design review comments, ensuring timely incorporation and closure. • Liaise directly with RTA Engineering, Traffic, and Safety departments during design development and approvals. • Ensure compliance with road safety audit requirements, traffic staging, and temporary traffic management design. • Support preparation and review of authority submissions, NOCs, and permits related to RTA projects. • Participate in design coordination meetings, technical workshops, and value engineering sessions. • Identify design risks and propose innovative, cost-effective, and constructible solutions. • Provide technical support during tender stage, including responding to queries, addenda, and clarifications. • Support construction teams by reviewing RFIs, design changes, and technical queries during execution. • Mentor junior engineers and ensure design deliverables meet quality, schedule, and RTA approval timelines. • Ensure proper document control and adherence to the project Design Management Plan and QA/QC procedures. • Contribute to preparation of as-built drawings and design closeout documentation. Minimum of 10 years of experience in the UAE Knowledge with the local authority procedure, standards and regulations in UAE is essential. Excellent interpersonal, communication and presentation skills. BEng or MEng in Civil engineering

jobs.title: Vice President, Strategy Pricing & Interchange
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Vice President, Strategy Pricing & Interchange You will be leading the economics strategy for Mastercard products and services for Eastern Europe Middle East and Africa Region (EEMEA). You will design, assess and implement pricing and interchange driven initiatives to strengthen Mastercard’s’ competitive position on product and country level. By enhancing and shaping the evolution of our economics you will play an important role in the development and adoption of innovative payment solutions. You will have broad exposure within the organization and work closely with Country, Division, and Functional management teams and liaise with partners in the Legal, Finance and Business Development teams. You will serve as strategic thought partner for senior management and contribute to key projects for Mastercard’s strategy. The role reports into Business Excellence Lead for EEMEA. Key Responsibilities: • Deliver holistic assessments on pricing and interchange initiatives along strategic, customer, financial impact, risk and feasibility angles. (e.g., run financial impact simulations on country, product and customer level or run competitive economics analysis) • Supervise cost studies that underpin the Interchange propositions (including members briefings and meetings) • Prepare recommendations on innovative new product business cases and necessary materials for decision making and present recommendations to Senior Management • Collaborate closely with regional and country management across EEMEA • Participating in cross-functional teams and liaising closely with various internal and external stakeholders, e.g., Product, Finance, Legal , Customers, consultants, etc. • Identifying and designing opportunities to support EEMEA objectives Key Skills & Experience: • Proven demonstration of top class problem solving skills • Excellent analytical skills: structuring and conducting rigorous analysis • Ability to think strategically and develop a strategic view on Mastercard’s services and economics- understanding the financial fundamentals and architecture behind Mastercard business • Excellent communication skills; able to liaise and work with a customer oriented mindset towards its internal and external counterparts (e.g., accountability, sense of urgency, excellence). • Strong drive and curiosity to learn, creativity, passion to achieve and excel • Ability to manage projects and use project-management skills for managing complex processes • Strong team player who can operate effectively in a complex matrix • Strong background in finance and analytics • Excellent knowledge of Agentic Automation, Excel, and other Information Management tools • Fluent in English Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

jobs.title: Product Integration, EEMEA (Turkish Speaker)
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Product Integration, EEMEA (Turkish Speaker) What we’re hiring for We are hiring an Integration Manager to support customers across Eastern Europe, Middle East and Africa as part of our Ethoca EEMEA Product Integration team. This role is critical to how our customers experience Mastercard and Ethoca. You will work directly with customers integrating our solutions, many of whom are technically sophisticated, highly demanding, and expect strong guidance throughout the process. You will also partner closely with our Sales teams to support pre‑sales technical discussions and solution design. This is a hands‑on, customer‑facing role that requires technical fluency, structured execution, and the ability to drive clarity across complex stakeholder environments. About the role The role has two core responsibilities: Customer integration & delivery Act as the primary technical point of contact for customers during onboarding and post‑go‑live. Lead customers through end‑to‑end integrations, from kick‑off through configuration, testing, and launch. Support customers that require close engagement, frequent follow‑ups, and clear technical guidance. Review customer requirements and existing processes and recommend practical, scalable solutions. Build and maintain integration plans, track risks and mitigations, and provide clear status updates to customers and internal stakeholders. Own progress and outcomes — identifying issues early and driving resolution. Pre‑sales technical support Partner with Sales during pre‑sales engagements, acting as the technical expert in customer discussions. Support solution design, feasibility assessments, and technical Q&A during the sales cycle. Translate customer needs into actionable technical requirements for internal teams. Across both areas, you will work closely with internal stakeholders to get answers, unblock issues, manage expectations, and keep work moving. Who the ideal candidate is The ideal candidate is pragmatic, proactive, and comfortable owning complex integrations. You: Have experience implementing or supporting API‑ or SFTP‑based integrations, ideally in fintech, payments, or SaaS. Are comfortable working directly with customers on technical topics and managing demanding stakeholders. Can explain technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences. Have strong execution discipline — you build plans, track risks, manage dependencies, and follow through. Proactively engage internal teams to get answers and move work forward. Are solution‑minded and focused on outcomes, not theory. Required Strong spoken and written Turkish & English Experience working with APIs Customer‑facing technical experience Ability to manage multiple integration efforts in parallel Willingness to be based in Dubai or Istanbul and support Middle East customers Nice to have Payments or financial services experience Experience with SQL or similar technical tools Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and Complete all periodic mandat …

jobs.title: Land Surveyor
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Land Surveyor to join our team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. As a Land Surveyor, you will play a crucial role in our infrastructure projects, ensuring precise measurements and alignments for various construction activities. Conduct topographic, control, and setting-out surveys for infrastructure projects, including stormwater drainage and microtunneling works Establish, verify, and maintain survey control points, benchmarks, and reference grids Review and apply approved drawings, alignment data, and coordinate systems in compliance with local standards and project requirements Perform precise measurements for horizontal and vertical alignments, pipe inverts, tunnel drives, and shaft locations Conduct as-built surveys and verify the accuracy of contractors' as-built drawings Monitor microtunneling drive alignments and breakthrough coordinates, ensuring compliance with allowable tolerances Maintain up-to-date survey records, field books, and digital data files Collaborate with contractors and supervision teams to resolve survey discrepancies or deviations on-site Verify and certify survey results for measurement sheets, interim payments, and progress verification Participate in pre-construction and progress meetings to provide input on survey status and required actions Ensure compliance with health and safety standards during all field operations Calibrate and maintain survey instruments according to project quality assurance procedures Provide support during testing, commissioning, and handover stages by verifying final levels and coordinates Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Surveying, Civil Engineering, or related discipline Minimum 7 years of experience in surveying and setting-out for infrastructure projects Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GPS, Total Station, and surveying software Strong understanding of coordinate systems, datum control, and vertical alignment verification Experience with deep pipeline, shaft construction, and tunnel alignment monitoring

jobs.title: Inspector - TBM Tunnels
jobs.description: Role Summary Responsible for inspecting and monitoring rail tunnel construction works to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, contract requirements, railway standards, and HSE regulations. Key Responsibilities Inspect rail tunnel works including TBM excavation, segmental lining, grouting, waterproofing, cross passages, shafts, track bed construction, and associated civil works. Monitor contractor activities to ensure compliance with approved method statements, ITPs, quality requirements, and construction sequences. Verify materials, workmanship, testing, and installation activities in accordance with project specifications and railway standards. Monitor TBM operations, segment erection, alignment, settlement monitoring, and ground movement control measures. Maintain daily site records and report on progress, quality issues, and non-conformities. Witness inspections, testing, commissioning, and handover activities. Coordinate with contractors, engineers, and client representatives to resolve site issues and ensure timely corrective actions. Ensure compliance with project HSE requirements and tunnel safety procedures. Minimum Requirements Diploma or Degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline. 7 to 10 years of construction inspection experience on major rail, metro, or underground infrastructure projects. Mandatory experience on TBM tunnel projects  Strong knowledge of tunnel construction, railway standards, QA/QC procedures, and HSE requirements. Experience on High Speed Rail or metro projects is preferred.     Diploma or Degree in Civil or Railway Engineering (or equivalent).

jobs.title: Quantity Surveyor (Roads and Infrastructure Experience)
jobs.description: • Normally receives little instruction on routine work, general instructions on new assignments.  • Conducts surveys to ascertain the locations of natural features and human-made structures on the earth's surface, underground, and underwater, using electronic distance-measuring equipment and other surveying instruments.  • Operates and manages land-information computer systems, performs tasks such as storing data, making inquiries, and producing plots and reports.  • Reviews information from survey teams regarding measurement of distances, directions, angles between points and elevation of points, lines, and contours on, above, and below the earth’s surface.  • Researches legal records, looks for evidence of previous boundaries, and analyzes the data to determine the location of boundary lines.  • Records the results of surveys, verifies the accuracy of data, and prepares plots, maps, and reports.  • An experienced professional with full understanding of area of specialization.  • Works on problems of diverse scope. Must have a QS or Engineering degree or similar equivalent Minimum 7 years of experience in quantity surveying for infrastructure projects Experience in roads and infrastructure projects  Specific Skills Required High level of literacy skills in English. Arabic speaking considered a benefit. Build successful working relationships with internal departments Ability to handle multiple tasks and establish priorities for the timely completion of work. High level of professionalism, initiative and creativity. Problem solving/lateral thinking skills.

jobs.title: Director - Business Development and Capture Management
jobs.description: We are seeking a Director of Business Development and Capture Management to join our organization in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. In this strategic leadership role, you will drive business growth through the identification, pursuit, and successful capture of high-value opportunities. You will lead cross-functional teams to develop compelling proposals, manage complex sales pipelines, and build lasting relationships with key stakeholders in the region.  Talent Developer Encourages diverse and inclusive collaboration leveraging AECOM’s global footprint to enable individual growth and collective success through trust, autonomy, accountability, and alignment. When developing others: Builds a pursuit team and succession plan for capture management at AECOM. Connects the pursuit team to AECOM bid strategies and ensures our current and potential clients get the best    AECOM experience for every pursuit. Coaches and supports other team members in building strong relationships across the organization. Actively supports the attraction and retention of capture management talent. Facilitates the growth and success of AECOM’s next generation of leaders by providing opportunities to         develop, such as cross border/business line experiences. jobs.title: Team Lead Sales - Dubai
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

About the Team
You will be part of a dynamic team composed of Careem experienced professionals to build and grow Careem Food to become the market leader. We strive to embody our values of ownership, shooting to the moon, and being of service to customers, partners, colleagues, and captains. As Sales Team Lead, you will manage the team responsible for executing our sales strategy and vision by successfully signing and onboarding new partners and improving the customer experience through increased restaurant supply.

About the Role
As the Sales Team Lead for Careem, you will be responsible for driving new restaurant partnerships across UAE, with a strong focus on Dubai. Your leadership will be instrumental in motivating and guiding our sales team to achieve ambitious growth targets. Your expertise in sales strategy, team management, and market insights will play a pivotal role in shaping our success across this key market.

What you'll do

Leadership and Team Management:

  • Lead, inspire, and manage a team of 8 sales professionals to achieve sales targets.
  • Provide coaching, training, and support for the team’s professional growth.
  • Support the team in closing deals and lead key negotiations where necessary.
  • Drive pipeline management and improve efficiency.

Sales Strategy and Planning:

  • Collaborate with the Head of Sales to develop and execute winning sales strategies for Dubai.
  • Identify and prioritize must-have brands and high-demand restaurant categories to close the competitive inventory gap.
  • Use creative, data-driven approaches to craft pitches and materials to close deals.
  • Implement and refine processes to enhance team performance.
  • Work cross-functionally to ensure successful onboarding and sustained partner growth.
  • Conduct regular competitive benchmarking to identify supply gaps versus key competitors.
  • Build and maintain a target list of priority brands and work relentlessly to bring them onto Careem Food.
  • Partner with internal teams (marketing, ops, account management) to ensure newly signed brands are set up for success and retained.

What you'll need

  • 3–5 years of experience managing sales teams with a proven track record in direct sales.
  • Experience transforming teams through strong strategy and management practices.
  • Demonstrated ability to build a high-performance culture and a unified team vision.
  • Proficiency in data analysis and tools such as Excel …

jobs.title: Reciprocating Compressors - Technical Sales Senior Advisor - New Units
jobs.description: Do you want to be part of a global team transforming the energy industry? Do you have experience in Reciprocating Compressors? Join our team! Baker Hughes stands as a leading global energy‑technology company, delivering innovative solutions that make energy safer, cleaner, and more efficient across the entire value chain. Operating in more than 120 countries, the company blends deep industrial expertise with advanced engineering and digital technologies to support energy production, transformation, and decarbonization. With over a century of heritage, Baker Hughes partners with customers in oil and gas, industrial sectors, and emerging energy markets to accelerate progress toward a more sustainable energy future. Partner with the best! The Reciprocating Compressor (RC) Technical Sales will be responsible for delivering aggressive orders and margin targets for new units’ business, working closely with the specific regional sales team. You will lead technical support to Sales/Regional commercial teams streamlining technical solutions for customers during the pre-bid phase, supporting the sales team with the assigned regional customers, aiding in identifying the best technical solutions to meet customer requirements as well as new possible solutions in their space. Leading early engagement with customers during pre-bid stage, to identify customer needs, influencing the final decision of the technical solution and converting it into solid demand generation for both greenfield and brownfield applications. Defining the technical scope of the solution and relevant execution model; coordinating cross functional support for developing budget proposals and feasibility studies. Securing solid, continuous, and easy exchange of information with the engineering/product management/manufacturing center of excellence and senior management to promote valuable solutions and quick decisions for the customers. Supporting Product Leadership from a technical standpoint in setting up commercial alliances and partnerships with customers for market development. Supporting Product Leadership in the definition of needed equipment portfolio, based on customer requirements and market expectations. Leading the technical qualification of New Products with customers and support commercialization strategy. Contributing to strategic planning process through Identification of potential NPD, raising product development challenges and assessing and promoting new product/solutions. Coordinating with the assigned Region(s) to finalize regional strategy for events and provide technical support as needed. To be successful in this role, you will have: Bachelor's degree from an accredited university or college. jobs.title: Land Surveyor
jobs.description: The Land Surveyor will be responsible for performing precise surveying and setting-out activities for stormwater and microtunneling works, including shafts, tunnels, pipelines, chambers, and related structures. The role ensures that all construction alignments, levels, and coordinates are established accurately and in accordance with approved drawings, project specifications, and RTA/DM standards. Perform topographic, control, and setting-out surveys for stormwater and microtunneling works, including pipelines, shafts, manholes, and associated civil structures. Establish, verify, and maintain survey control points, benchmarks, and reference grids in coordination with the contractor's survey team, applying approved IFC drawings and coordinate systems in compliance with RTA and project requirements. Check and verify horizontal and vertical alignments, pipe inverts, tunnel drives, shaft locations, and microtunneling drive alignments, ensuring compliance with allowable tolerances prior to and during construction. Ensure that all setting-out activities are carried out in accordance with approved method statements and calibration procedures. Conduct as-built surveys and verify the accuracy of contractors' as-built drawings and survey results prior to submission and certification for interim payments. Maintain up-to-date survey records, field books, and digital data files, ensuring full traceability of survey operations. Coordinate with the contractor and supervision team to resolve survey discrepancies or deviations on-site, and participate in pre-construction and progress meetings to provide input on survey status and required actions. Ensure full compliance with HSE standards during all field operations, particularly in confined-space and shaft areas. Provide support during testing, commissioning, and handover stages by verifying final levels and coordinates. Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Surveying, Civil Engineering, or related discipline. RTA approval and certification for Microtunneling Works is mandatory. Certification in surveying instruments and data management is an advantage. Proficient in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GPS, Total Station, and surveying software. Familiar with RTA, DM, and DEWA standards for utility and infrastructure projects.

jobs.title: Land Surveyor
jobs.description: The Land Surveyor will be responsible for performing precise surveying and setting-out activities for stormwater and microtunneling works, including shafts, tunnels, pipelines, chambers, and related structures. The role ensures that all construction alignments, levels, and coordinates are established accurately and in accordance with approved drawings, project specifications, and RTA/DM standards. Perform topographic, control, and setting-out surveys for stormwater and microtunneling works, including pipelines, shafts, manholes, and associated civil structures. Establish, verify, and maintain survey control points, benchmarks, and reference grids in coordination with the contractor’s survey team. Review and apply approved IFC drawings, alignment data, and coordinate systems in compliance with RTA and project requirements. Check and verify horizontal and vertical alignments, pipe inverts, tunnel drives, and shaft locations prior to and during construction. Ensure that all setting-out activities are carried out in accordance with approved method statements and calibration procedures. Conduct as-built surveys and verify the accuracy of contractors’ as-built drawings prior to submission. Proficient in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GPS, Total Station, and surveying software. Familiar with RTA, DM, and DEWA standards for utility and infrastructure projects.

jobs.title: Engineer - Utilities
jobs.description: Supervise and inspect the construction of dry utility networks including HV/LV cables, street lighting, telecom (Etisalat/Du), ITS, and other related infrastructure to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and authority standards (RTA, DEWA, Etisalat, Du, and DM). Review excavation, duct laying, backfilling, and cable pulling works to verify compliance with project specifications and approved method statements. Monitor and verify jointing, termination, and chamber installation for electrical and telecom networks in accordance with approved standards. Ensure proper coordination and interface between dry and wet utility networks to avoid clashes and ensure smooth implementation. Inspect trench preparation, bedding, and protection layers before and after utility installation. Review and comment on shop drawings, material submittals, method statements, and as-built drawings related to dry utilities. Monitor contractor’s work to ensure compliance with safety requirements, quality control procedures, and approved ITPs (Inspection and Test Plans). Witness and verify inspection requests (WIRs) and ensure all tests such as continuity, insulation resistance, and functional testing are properly conducted and documented. Coordinate with local authorities (RTA, DEWA, Etisalat, Du, Civil Defense, and DM) for inspections, NOCs, and approvals. Participate in progress and coordination meetings to discuss technical issues, utility conflicts, and schedule updates. Verify trench reinstatement and surface finishing works are carried out as per project and authority requirements. Ensure the contractor provides adequate protection and marking of utilities after installation. Support the Resident Engineer and Utility Engineer in resolving site issues and verifying as-built information before handover. Ensure all testing, commissioning, and handover documentation for dry utilities are complete and in compliance with project closeout requirements Bachelor's degree in civil engineering or related field Minimum of 7-12 years of experience in Utilities engineering. Masters is prefrable but not mandatory

jobs.title: Civil Inspector - (rails/ tracks)
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Civil Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for conducting comprehensive inspections of railway tracks and rail infrastructure at our freight facilities, ensuring compliance with safety standards and operational excellence. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect rail and track construction activities to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and quality standards. Monitor installation of rail tracks, sleepers, ballast, fastening systems, and related civil works. Verify track alignment, levels, gauge, cant, and geometry as per approved tolerances. Inspect earthworks, subgrade preparation, formation layers, and concrete works related to railway infrastructure. Ensure proper execution of welding works, rail joints, thermite welding, and stress adjustment activities. Conduct inspection of track slab construction, reinforcement, embedded items, and concrete pouring activities where applicable. Review shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and inspection test plans (ITPs). Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects, particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures, including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Director, Strategy and M&A Mastercard is seeking a Director, Strategy, based in Dubai, UAE. This role is pivotal in shaping and driving Mastercard's strategic growth initiatives across Eastern Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EEMEA). This role will lead identifying strategic growth opportunities, aligned with Mastercard’s strategic priorities in the EEMEA region. Key Responsibilities • Lead the development and execution of the strategic plan for EEMEA, directly shaping regional, corporate strategy and priorities • Oversee and drive high-priority strategic projects, structuring complex deals, conducting rigorous analyses, and delivering actionable insights and recommendations to the senior leadership team • Lead the regional localization agenda and drive the strategy to expand share in local payment flows • Monitor industry trends and competitive landscape, proactively sharing insightful analyses and reports with the broader team and senior executives • Lead strategic and financial analyses to identify and evaluate growth opportunities, market trends, competitive positioning, and operational performance • Engage and build relationships at the executive level both internally and externally to influence and drive strategic outcomes All About You • Extensive combined experience in Strategy within the payments and financial services sectors is a must • Deep understanding and experience within payments and financial services and related industries is a must • Proven track record of successfully leading large-scale, complex, cross-functional strategic projects in global, matrixed organizations • Demonstrated capability in strategic planning, advanced analytics, financial modeling, transaction valuation, and risk assessment, hands-on proficiency with analytics platforms • Exceptional executive-level communication and presentation skills, adept at influencing diverse stakeholder groups and driving strategic alignment • Strong leadership skills with experience building, motivating, and managing high-performing teams in a dynamic, matrixed organization • Experience of managing multiple stakeholders in a multicultural environment, across multiple geographic locations. Strong negotiating and influencing skills, with the ability to manage delivery through others who do not report directly. • Ability to work collaboratively and both independently and in a team environment. • Excellent writing and presentation skills, extensive experience in communicating in international environment at senior management level • 7+ years of progressive business experience and demonstrated results in strategic, financial and/or commercially focused roles. Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

jobs.title: Manager - Electrical
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: We are hiring an experienced Electrical Manager to lead all electrical supervision activities on a construction project, ensuring technical compliance, safety, quality, and timely delivery. The role will manage a team of electrical engineers/inspectors, interface with the client, contractor, designers, and authorities, and drive resolution of technical issues from pre-construction through testing, commissioning, and handover. Key Responsibilities Technical Leadership & Design Assurance • Lead technical reviews of shop drawings, material submittals, method statements, ITPs, and as-builts for compliance with contract, IFC drawings, and applicable codes/standards (e.g., IEC, IEEE, NEC, BS, local codes). • Provide authoritative responses to RFIs/technical queries and maintain a robust decision log. • Verify electrical calculations (load, short-circuit, protection coordination, volt drop, earthing/grounding, lighting, cable sizing) and propose optimizations where appropriate. Construction Supervision & Quality Control • Plan, assign, and oversee daily inspections (WIR/MIR), surveillance, and hold/witness points; manage NCRs, CARs, and closeouts. • Monitor installation of LV/MV/HV distribution, substations/switchgear, transformers, generators, UPS, earthing and lightning protection, lighting, small power, and ELV systems (fire alarm, PA/VA, BMS, CCTV, access control, structured cabling). • Ensure proper storage, handling, and installation per manufacturer requirements and project specifications. • Validate temporary electrical works and energization plans for safe construction staging. Testing, Commissioning & Handover • Lead FAT/SAT, pre-commissioning, commissioning, and integrated systems testing; approve procedures and verify test records. • Coordinate protection settings, relay testing, SCADA/BMS integration, and utility interfaces up to energization. • Oversee readiness for handover: snag/punch lists, O&M manuals, asset registers, training, spares, warranties, and final as-builts. Interface, Stakeholder & Authority Coordination • Coordinate with civil/MEP disciplines to resolve clashes and sequence works; drive BIM-based coordination when applicable. • Liaise with client representatives, designers, utilities, and authorities to secure NOCs, permits, inspections, and energization approvals. • Chair electrical progress/coordination meetings; issue clear action plans and minutes. Schedule, Cost & Contract Administration • Track electrical progress vs. baseline; review look-aheads and recovery plans; validate measurement and earned value for electrical scope. • Support change management: evaluate contractor claims/variations for technical merit and impact; produce recommendations. • Administer contract requirements (e.g., FIDIC or equivalent): deliverables, notices, approvals, and close-out. Minimum Qualifications • Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering (BSc/BEng) from an accredited institution. • 15+ years’ experience in electrical engineering with at least 5 years in construction supervision consultancy and 2–3 years in a lead/management role. • Proven delivery on medium to large-scale building, infrastructure, or industrial projects. • Strong knowledge of relevant codes/standards (IEC/IEEE/NEC/BS) and local authority requirements. • Demonstrated experience with testing, commissioning, and utility/authority energization processes. • Excellent English communication (written and verbal); strong report writing and stakeholder management. Preferred Qualifications & Certifications • Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or related discipline. • Professional registration (e.g., CEng, PE) and/or PMP. • Chartered membership or active affiliation with IEEE/IES/IEI (or equivalent). • Prior experience with FIDIC-based contracts. • Regional authority experience (e.g., utilities/municipal approvals) for the project location.

You will oversee complex projects from conceptualization through completion, ensuring all designs meet international standards, local regulations, and client expectations while maintaining budgetary and timeline constraints. Lead the architectural design and planning of stadium and sports facility projects, from initial concept through final construction documentation Develop comprehensive design solutions that integrate structural integrity, aesthetic appeal, crowd management, and operational efficiency Manage project timelines, budgets, and resource allocation while maintaining quality standards and regulatory compliance Collaborate with engineers, contractors, consultants, and stakeholders to ensure seamless project execution and coordination Conduct thorough analysis of building codes, safety regulations, architectural plans and visualizations Mentor and supervise junior architects and design team members, fostering professional development and design excellence Prepare and present design proposals, assessments and feasibility studies to inform design decisions and project planning Ensure all designs account for venue acoustics, spectator comfort, accessibility, and emergency egress requirements Minimum 10+ years of professional experience in architectural design, with at least 5 years focused on stadium or large-scale sports facility projects Demonstrated expertise in stadium architecture, venue design, and sports facility planning Advanced proficiency with architectural design software (AutoCAD, Revit, SketchUp) and BIM methodologies Strong knowledge of building codes, safety standards, accessibility regulations, and local UAE construction requirements Proven experience managing large-scale projects from design through construction administration Excellent project management skills with demonstrated ability to manage budgets, timelines, and multiple stakeholders Strong leadership capabilities with experience mentoring and supervising architectural teams

jobs.title: Senior Auditor - Corporate/Commercial Banking - Assistant Vice President
jobs.description: Are you looking for a career move that will put you at the heart of a global financial institution? Then bring your skills in analysis, problem solving and communication to Citi’s Internal Audit International team. By Joining Citi, you will become part of a global organisation whose mission is to serve as a trusted partner to our clients by responsibly providing financial services that enable growth and economic progress. Team/Role Overview The Senior Auditor is responsible for delivering audit assurance activities with primary coverage of Corporate/Commercial Banking, while also participating in Citibank UAE cross-discipline audits. What you’ll do Participate in the Citibank UAE audits including drafting audit reports, presenting issues to the business, and discussing practical and sustainable solutions. Monitor regulatory developments to ensure audit coverage addresses applicable regulatory requirements. Conduct audit and regulatory issue validations, risk assessments and business monitoring. Adhere to Internal Audit standards, policies, and regulatory requirements while providing timely and effective audit assurance; ensure established timelines and budgets are met. Apply professional judgement in evaluating risks and forming audit conclusions, escalate significant risks accordingly. Contribute to continuous improvement of audit assurance processes, including development of data analytics and usage of AI tools to enhance audit efficiency and coverage. Develop and maintain effective relationships with stakeholders to ensure strong understanding of the business. What we’ll need from you 5-8 years of relevant experience in a related role Solid understanding of Corporate/Commercial Banking, and the related regulatory environment Related certifications (CPA, ACA, CFA, CIA, CISA, ACAMS or similar) preferred Effective verbal and written communication and negotiation skills Effective project management skills Effective influencing and relationship management skills Ability to remain unbiased in a diverse working environment By joining Citi Dubai, you will not only be part of a business casual workplace with a hybrid working model (up to 2 days working at home per week), but also receive a competitive base salary (which is annually reviewed) and enjoy a whole host of additional benefits that support you (and your family) to be well, live well and save well. Alongside these benefits Citi is committed to ensuring our workplace is where everyone feels comfortable coming to work as their whole self every day. We want the best talent around the world to be energized to join us, motivated to stay, and empowered to thrive. Sounds like Citi has everything you need? Then apply to discover the true extent of your capabilities. #LI-MP4 ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family Group: Internal Audit ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family: Audit ------------------------------------------------------ Time Type: Full time ------------------------------------------------------ Most Relevant Skills Control Monitoring, Data Analysis, Industry Knowledge, Issue Management, Laws and Regulations, Professional Standards, Risk Management, Root Cause Analysis. ------------------------------------------------------ Other Relevant Skills For complementary skills, please see above and/or contact the recruiter. ------------------------------------------------------ Citi is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristic protected by law. If you are a person with a disability and need a reasonable accommodation to use our search tools and/or apply for a career opportunity review Accessibility at Citi . View Citi’s EEO Policy Statement and the Know Your Rights poster.

jobs.title: Product Integration, EEMEA
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Product Integration, EEMEA What we’re hiring for We are hiring an Integration Manager to support customers across Eastern Europe, Middle East and Africa as part of our Ethoca EEMEA Product Integration team. This role is critical to how our customers experience Mastercard and Ethoca. You will work directly with customers integrating our solutions, many of whom are technically sophisticated, highly demanding, and expect strong guidance throughout the process. You will also partner closely with our Sales teams to support pre‑sales technical discussions and solution design. This is a hands‑on, customer‑facing role that requires technical fluency, structured execution, and the ability to drive clarity across complex stakeholder environments. About the role The role has two core responsibilities: Customer integration & delivery Act as the primary technical point of contact for customers during onboarding and post‑go‑live. Lead customers through end‑to‑end integrations, from kick‑off through configuration, testing, and launch. Support customers that require close engagement, frequent follow‑ups, and clear technical guidance. Review customer requirements and existing processes and recommend practical, scalable solutions. Build and maintain integration plans, track risks and mitigations, and provide clear status updates to customers and internal stakeholders. Own progress and outcomes — identifying issues early and driving resolution. Pre‑sales technical support Partner with Sales during pre‑sales engagements, acting as the technical expert in customer discussions. Support solution design, feasibility assessments, and technical Q&A during the sales cycle. Translate customer needs into actionable technical requirements for internal teams. Across both areas, you will work closely with internal stakeholders to get answers, unblock issues, manage expectations, and keep work moving. Who the ideal candidate is The ideal candidate is pragmatic, proactive, and comfortable owning complex integrations. You: Have experience implementing or supporting API‑ or SFTP‑based integrations, ideally in fintech, payments, or SaaS. Are comfortable working directly with customers on technical topics and managing demanding stakeholders. Can explain technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences. Have strong execution discipline — you build plans, track risks, manage dependencies, and follow through. Proactively engage internal teams to get answers and move work forward. Are solution‑minded and focused on outcomes, not theory. Required Strong spoken and written English Experience working with APIs Customer‑facing technical experience Ability to manage multiple integration efforts in parallel Willingness to be based in Dubai or India and support Middle East customers Nice to have Arabic language skills Payments or financial services experience Experience with SQL or similar technical tools Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must: Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices; Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed; Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and Complete all periodic mandatory secu …

jobs.title: Midstream Facilities Engineer - Mature Assets
jobs.description: Are you excited to plan, organize, and implement continuous improvement methods? Would you like to take ownership of the strategy and execution of Carbon Out methods? About the team A small highly skilled team, capable of delivering innovative solutions for complex integrity management challenges in challenging environments. The role is a key position within the diverse, multi-disciplined, results focused team. The successful candidate shall meet the technical requirements listed below whilst being focused on collaborating with internal and external resources to deliver on strategic goals. Partner with the best As a Midtsream Facilities Engineer – Mature Asset, you will be responsible for the maintenance activities or new installation of electrical and mechanical systems in Bari site of related facilities equipment. In this role, you will be responsible for: Defining high-level upstream and midstream concepts (wellsite, CPF, compression, treating, water handling, export). Identify processing, compression, power, water disposal, storage, and export limitations. Providing screening-level CAPEX/OPEX estimates and key cost drivers. Experience in utilizing QUESTOR a plus. Assessing gathering systems, pipelines, third-party access, tariffs, and specification constraints. Identifying facilities-related risks for the asset risk register (sour service, emissions, integrity, permitting, brownfield complexity). Reviewing facilities documentation, integrity records, and brownfield tie-in constraints. Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: BSc in Chemical, Mechanical, or Process Engineering. Have 15+ years of experience in upstream and/or midstream facilities engineering. Strong background in concept selection, brownfield modifications, cost estimating, and process safety. Experience supporting asset evaluations and transactions. Possess excellent oral and written communication skills Demonstrate effective problem identification and solution skills on complex projects Show proven analytical and organizational ability Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities Comprehensive private medical care options Safety net of life insurance and disability programs Tailored financial programs Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Engineering Senior Manager - Process Engineering

jobs.title: Inspector - Civil
jobs.description: Ensure contractor compliance with project standards, specifications, and approved drawings through regular site inspections and audits Maintain comprehensive daily records of contractor activities, manpower, equipment, and site progress Undertake site supervision and measurements (pipe alignments, levels, elevations) as instructed by the Resident Engineer (RE) or Assistant Resident Engineer (ARE) Apply and enforce quality control and quality assurance procedures on site, monitoring contractor QA/QC activities and maintaining relevant records Prepare daily reports for the RE or ARE, including work orders, materials delivery, inspection activities, and identified site issues Issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) and Site Observation Reports (SORs) for deviations, and follow up on corrective actions Review and provide comments on Requests for Information (RFIs) and technical submittals from contractors Monitor contractor progress against approved schedules and report delays or potential slippages to the RE or ARE Liaise daily with the contractor to ensure effective management of resources, health and safety, and equipment Ensure all site works comply with health, safety, and environmental (HSE) requirements and local authority regulations (RTA, DM, DEWA, etc.) Coordinate with other disciplines (structural, electrical, mechanical) to ensure proper integration of civil works Participate in site meetings, coordination sessions, and technical discussions with relevant stakeholders Conduct final inspections and punch list verification prior to project milestones and handover stages Maximum of 8-10 years of experience. BE in Civil engineering with equivalency certificate is mandatory

jobs.title: Site Architect
jobs.location: Ras Al Khaimah, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: A Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. Key Responsibilities: Site Supervision: Oversee the construction site to ensure that the architectural design is implemented correctly and according to the plans. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of materials and workmanship to ensure compliance with design specifications and standards. Coordination: Collaborate with contractors, engineers, and other professionals to resolve any design or construction issues. Compliance: Ensure that all construction activities comply with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental guidelines. Problem-Solving: Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise on-site, making adjustments to plans as necessary. Reporting: Prepare and present progress reports to clients and stakeholders, highlighting any deviations from the plan and proposed solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of site activities, including daily logs, inspection reports, and change orders. Client Interaction: Liaise with clients to understand their needs and ensure their satisfaction with the project. Education: Bachelor's degree in Architecture, Master's degree preferred. Experience: Minimum of 10 years of experience in architecture and construction management, with experience gained working on site and in a construction supervision capacity. Experience of working on large scale horizontal building projects is also desired

jobs.title: Senior Planning/Project Controls Engineer
jobs.description: We are seeking a seasoned Project Planning/Project Controls Engineer to lead schedule and cost controls across complex infrastructure and buildings programs.You will shape robust baselines, drive project performance, and provide clear insights that help our clients make confident decisions. Ensure project schedules comply with RTA specifications, guidelines, and standard work breakdown structures (WBS) for roads, bridges, and infrastructure projects. Coordinate planning activities in line with RTA approval procedures, including interim milestones, traffic staging, and sectional handover requirements. Monitor and integrate RTA authority approvals (NOCs, permits, traffic diversion approvals, utility coordination) into the project schedule. Review and assess contractors’ programs against RTA contractual milestones, key performance indicators (KPIs), and phasing requirements. Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports. Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Quantity Surveying, or related field (Master’s preferred). Senior planning/controls role requiring 15+ years’ experience, strong base cost and standard planning capability, consultancy and local market experience, and a proven track record across infrastructure and buildings. Advanced proficiency in Primavera P6, cost control, EVM, risk analysis, and executive‑quality reporting is essential. Client‑facing leadership, clear communication, and rigorous governance will be central to success.

jobs.title: Inspector - Utilties (Rails/Tracks)
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Utilities Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect and monitor all civil utilities works on site to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and authority standards. Supervise installation activities related to utilities such as stormwater drainage, sewerage, potable water, irrigation, telecom, electrical duct banks, and utility corridors. Verify line and level, pipe bedding, backfilling, compaction, and trench works before proceeding with subsequent activities. Ensure materials delivered to site are approved and comply with project specifications and quality standards. Conduct inspections for excavation, pipe laying, manholes, chambers, thrust blocks, and utility crossings. Review and monitor contractor’s work progress in accordance with approved method statements and construction schedules. Coordinate with surveyors and contractors to ensure utilities are installed at correct alignment and elevations. Raise NCRs, site observations, and non-conformance reports for any deviations from specifications or approved drawings. Witness testing activities including hydrostatic tests, pressure tests, leakage tests, compaction tests, and CCTV inspections where applicable. Ensure proper implementation of approved shop drawings and IFC drawings during construction activities. Monitor health, safety, and environmental compliance during utility installation activities. Coordinate with utility authorities and consultants for inspections, approvals, and clearance requirements. Maintain daily inspection records, site diaries, and quality documentation related to utilities works. Verify reinstatement works for roads, pavements, and affected areas after utility installation. Attend progress meetings, technical discussions, and coordination meetings with contractors and stakeholders. Ensure all WIRs (Work Inspection Requests) are inspected and closed within the required timeframe. Review as-built drawings and verify completed utility works before handover. Monitor subcontractor performance and ensure workmanship meets project quality requirements. Support the Resident Engineer and Civil Engineer in resolving site technical issues and construction conflicts. Ensure utility works comply with relevant authority requirements. Prepare snag lists and verify closure of defects prior to project completion and handover Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent professional qualification (HND/Diploma in Civil Engineering acceptable) Minimum 7 years of professional experience in utilities, infrastructure, or rail projects Proven experience on UAE-based rail projects with knowledge of local construction standards and regulatory requirements Proficiency in reading, interpreting, and enforcing compliance with technical drawings, specifications, and contract documents Experience preparing and maintaining inspection logs, test reports, and site documentation in accordance with contract procedures Familiarity with Non-Conformance Reporting (NCR) and Site Observation Report (SOR) procedures Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) certification or demonstrated commitment to HSE best practices Proficiency with construction management software and digital record-keeping platforms Excellent communication and stakeholder coordination skills with proven ability to liaise with contractors, consultants, and utility authorities Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with ability to resolve technical issues independently Meticulous attention to detail with proven track record of maintaining accurate site records and ensuring specification compliance

jobs.title: Senior Engineer - QA/QC
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: Job Summary: The QA/QC Engineer is responsible for ensuring that all project activities, materials, and workmanship comply with applicable quality standards, specifications, and client requirements. The role involves monitoring, inspecting, and reporting on quality-related aspects throughout the project lifecycle to ensure successful project delivery.   Key Responsibilities: Develop, implement, and maintain the Project Quality Management Plan (QMP).
