Review and approve Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs), method statements, and quality procedures. Coordinate with project teams, consultants, and contractors on all quality-related matters. Perform internal audits and participate in external audits as required. Ensure proper calibration and control of measuring and testing equipment. Need experience in Marine Projects  Support continuous improvement initiatives related to quality performance. Qualifications & Experience: Minimum of ten (10) years of experience in QA/QC within similar projects. Proven experience in quality control and assurance practices in construction/engineering projects. Familiarity with international standards such as ISO 9001. Skills & Competencies: Strong knowledge of QA/QC procedures, standards, and documentation. Experience with inspection, testing, and auditing processes. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and coordination abilities. Proficiency in reporting and documentation tools

Maintain daily inspection records, site diaries, and quality documentation related to utilities works. Verify reinstatement works for roads, pavements, and affected areas after utility installation. Attend progress meetings, technical discussions, and coordination meetings with contractors and stakeholders. Ensure all WIRs (Work Inspection Requests) are inspected and closed within the required timeframe. Review as-built drawings and verify completed utility works before handover. Monitor subcontractor performance and ensure workmanship meets project quality requirements. Support the Resident Engineer and Civil Engineer in resolving site technical issues and construction conflicts. Ensure utility works comply with relevant authority requirements. Prepare snag lists and verify closure of defects prior to project completion and handover   Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering or equivalent professional qualification (HND/Diploma in Civil Engineering acceptable) Minimum 7 years of professional experience in utilities, infrastructure, or rail projects Proven experience on UAE-based rail projects with knowledge of local construction standards and regulatory requirements Proficiency in reading, interpreting, and enforcing compliance with technical drawings, specifications, and contract documents Experience preparing and maintaining inspection logs, test reports, and site documentation in accordance with contract procedures Familiarity with Non-Conformance Reporting (NCR) and Site Observation Report (SOR) procedures Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) certification or demonstrated commitment to HSE best practices Proficiency with construction management software and digital record-keeping platforms Excellent communication and stakeholder coordination skills with proven ability to liaise with contractors, consultants, and utility authorities Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with ability to resolve technical issues independently Meticulous attention to detail with proven track record of maintaining accurate site records and ensuring specification compliance

jobs.title: Land surveyor
jobs.description: Job Summary We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Land Surveyor to join our team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. As a Land Surveyor, you will play a crucial role in our infrastructure projects, ensuring precise measurements and alignments for various construction activities. Review and understand project contract documents, drawings, specifications, and relevant authority standards to ensure accurate survey execution and compliance with design requirements. Establish, verify, and maintain primary horizontal and vertical control points on site, including reference benchmarks and traverse networks. Coordinate with the Contractor’s surveyor to verify site benchmarks, layout, and control data prior to commencement of construction activities. Check and verify all setting-out works including alignment, levels, and coordinates for roads, utilities (wet and dry), structures, and other civil works to ensure accuracy and compliance with approved drawings. Monitor excavation levels, backfilling, and compaction layers to ensure compliance with approved design grades and tolerances. Inspect and confirm the correct installation of utilities, chambers, and ducts based on approved survey data and ensure correct cover levels and horizontal alignment. Conduct topographic and as-built surveys, ensuring data accuracy and proper submission of as-built drawings for approval. Verify survey instruments are properly calibrated and maintained as per project and QA/QC requirements. Assist the Resident Engineer (RE) and Assistant Resident Engineer (ARE) in checking the contractor’s survey works, identifying discrepancies, and issuing site instructions as required. Record and document survey data, prepare survey reports, and maintain field books for all inspection and verification activities. Ensure the contractor’s survey team follows approved method statements and safety procedures during survey activities. Perform joint surveys with the contractor and client representatives for measurement verification, payment quantities, and handover purposes. Ensure all utilities and structures are constructed to correct levels and alignments before covering or backfilling works. Participate in resolving survey-related issues or conflicts on site, including interface coordination between different packages or contractors. Report any discrepancies, deviations, or errors found in the contractor’s setting out to the RE/ARE immediately. Monitor Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) compliance during survey activities and report any unsafe practices observed on site. Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Surveying, Civil Engineering, or related discipline Minimum 7 years of experience in surveying and setting-out for infrastructure projects Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GPS, Total Station, and surveying software Strong understanding of coordinate systems, datum control, and vertical alignment verification Experience with deep pipeline, shaft construction, and tunnel alignment monitoring RTA approval and certification for Microtunneling Works (mandatory) Familiarity with RTA, DM, and DEWA standards for utility and infrastructure projects Expertise in stormwater drainage and microtunneling works Knowledge of UAE construction standards and procedures, particularly in Dubai Strong mathematical and problem-solving skills Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in all aspects of work Good physical fitness for fieldwork activities Valid driver's license Relevant university or college qualification (Min 3 Years of Diploma in Civil or Survey) RTA/ DM  Approval is preferred

jobs.title: Manager - Bids
jobs.description: Job Summary     Manages the end-to-end bid process for bids from identification to award. Leads the development of technical and commercial proposals for the Middle East Civil Infrastructure business. Leads bid development, as well as hands-on cost proposal development. Works in partnership with senior leadership to develop winning pursuit strategies, ensuring all elements are incorporated into proposals Assists in necessary market research and reporting required for new growth areas. Works with other team members and marketing staff to ensure that prerequisites (like prequalification or getting on a vendor list) are fulfilled on time. Prepares reports and presentations for internal and external use. Participates in the go/no-go decision-making process, along with the appropriate managers and participates in the bid development process from start to finish. Reviews tender documents, bid/qualifications request documents and drawings for proposal preparation. Works with the Discipline Leads to produce man-hour estimates, fees and delivery schedules. Resolves issues in any discussions with other End Markets and Regions as necessary to ensure that the RFP scope is addressed adequately and all costs are accurately considered. Oversees the process of obtaining quotes from sub-consultants as required and leads negotiations of appropriate terms and conditions based on AECOM's standard contract and Risk committee requirements. Monitors Salesforce entries for the business to ensure that the information entered is up to date and accurate. Ensures that AECOM policies and procedures are followed and that all records are maintained. Ensures that approvals are in place in line with the latest AECOM Delegations of Authority and all AECO governance processes are followed. Verifies that all legal qualifications and the Middle East Risk Committee directives have been included in the final bid document before submission to the client. Delivers within proposal deadlines. Prepares presentations required for the Risk Committee. Independently presents to the internal Risk committee for pursuits valued up to $50m. Manages and mentors junior staff A relevant degree qualification

jobs.title: Planning Engineer (Roads and Infrastructure)
jobs.description: Ensure project schedules comply with RTA specifications, guidelines, and standard work breakdown structures (WBS) for roads, bridges, and infrastructure projects. ·         Coordinate planning activities in line with RTA approval procedures, including interim milestones, traffic staging, and sectional handover requirements. ·          Monitor and integrate RTA authority approvals (NOCs, permits, traffic diversion approvals, utility coordination) into the project schedule. • Review and assess contractors’ programs against RTA contractual milestones, key performance indicators (KPIs), and phasing requirements. ·         Develop, review, and maintain detailed project schedules (baseline, recovery, and revised programs) using Primavera P6 or equivalent planning software. ·         Prepare master schedules, look-ahead programs, and weekly/monthly progress reports in coordination with project teams. ·         Monitor project progress against the approved baseline and identify delays, risks, and critical path impacts. ·         Perform schedule risk analysis, delay analysis, and time impact analysis (TIA) in accordance with contract requirements. ·         Coordinate with construction, procurement, design, and commercial teams to ensure alignment between schedule, resources, and deliverables. ·         Review contractors’ planning submissions, method statements, and manpower histograms for compliance with contract specifications. ·         Support the Project Manager/Resident Engineer by providing planning inputs for decision-making and corrective actions. ·         Prepare and evaluate EOT (Extension of Time) claims, substantiating delays with proper planning narratives and evidence. ·         Track procurement, long-lead items, approvals, and permits to ensure schedule compliance. ·         Develop recovery and mitigation plans to bring delayed activities back on track. ·         Conduct weekly and monthly planning meetings with stakeholders and issue meeting minutes related to schedule actions. ·         Ensure integration of cost, cash flow, and resource-loaded schedules where required. ·         Maintain proper documentation and ensure planning records comply with project controls and QA/QC procedures. ·         Provide mentoring and guidance to junior planning engineers and site planners. ·         Liaise with clients, consultants, and authorities on all schedule-related matters. ·         Support project close-out by preparing as-built programs and final planning reports. A minimum of 8+ years’ experience in the Planning / Project Controls field is required. Applicants would ideally have worked on major programs in transport. A Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or Construction Management. Advanced knowledge of ORACLE Primavera P6 Project Management Software. Ability to develop, monitor and control schedules. Hands on working knowledge of WBS coding structure, cost /schedule variance analysis, Earned Value Management (EVMS), project trend, contract change and claims. Working knowledge of Time Impact Analysis techniques.

Applicants would ideally have worked on major programs in transport. A Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or Construction Management. Advanced knowledge of ORACLE Primavera P6 Project Management Software. Ability to develop, monitor and control schedules. jobs.title: Inspector - Tunnels
jobs.description: Role Summary Responsible for inspecting and monitoring rail tunnel construction activities to ensure full compliance with approved drawings, specifications, contract requirements, applicable railway standards, and health, safety, and environmental regulations. Key Responsibilities Conduct site inspections of rail tunnel works including excavation, segmental lining, waterproofing, secondary lining, track bed, and associated civil works. Monitor contractor activities to ensure compliance with approved method statements, construction sequences, and quality procedures. Verify materials, workmanship, equipment installation, and testing activities in accordance with project requirements and rail industry standards. Ensure compliance with railway-specific safety requirements, including safe access/egress, permits to work, and tunnel working procedures. Maintain detailed daily site records including labour, plant, progress, inspections, test results, and non-conformance reports. Review and report on construction progress, quality issues, and site non-conformities to the Resident Engineer (RE) and Assistant Resident Engineer (ARE). Assist in verifying tunnel alignment, levels, surveying checks, and as-built documentation. Coordinate with contractors, engineers, and client representatives during inspections, testing, and commissioning activities. Monitor and witness testing, commissioning, and handover activities, ensuring all required documentation is completed and closed out. Support the RE/ARE in resolving site issues and ensuring timely corrective and preventive actions. Ensure adherence to project HSE requirements and participate in safety inspections, audits, and toolbox talks. Experience 7–10 years’ experience in construction inspection on major rail infrastructure projects. Proven experience in tunnel works within rail, metro, or underground transit projects. Strong knowledge of tunnelling methods, segmental lining, rail standards, QA/QC procedures, and HSE requirements in live rail environments.

jobs.title: Binance Accelerator Program - UX Researcher (Non-technical)
jobs.description: Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world. About Binance Accelerator Program Binance Accelerator Program is a concise 3-6 months program designed to have an immersive experience in the rapidly expanding Web3 space. You will be given the opportunity to experience life at Binance and understand what goes on behind the scenes of the worlds’ leading blockchain ecosystem. Alongside your job, there will also be a focus on networking and development, which will expand your professional network and build transferable skills to propel you forward in your career. You will be hired under a junior fixed term contract. Who may apply Currently a Bachelors students who can work 5 days per week for a period of 6 months. Team Introduction The Central Product Team at Binance is where innovation meets user experience. We're shaping the core platforms and services that support Binance’s global ecosystem, optimizing user interactions, and enhancing operational efficiency. If you're passionate about building scalable, secure solutions that drive crypto adoption, join us and be part of a team that's redefining the digital asset experience.

The right person here will define how Binance is understood by the next billion users.

jobs.title: Senior Managing Consultant, AML Subject Matter Expert, Advisors & Consulting Services
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Senior Managing Consultant, AML Subject Matter Expert, Advisors & Consulting Services Overview: The consulting services team at Mastercard is look for a senior candidate to deliver AML advisory services to our customers globally. The role, which is part of the consulting delivery team involves providing expert guidance to organizations on managing anti-money laundering (AML) risks, drive thought Leadership and in close collaboration with global product team, shape and support our Go To Market Strategy for AML Services. The candidate will have the opportunity to work with a high performing team across the organization (sales, global product, internal compliance teams) and become an integral part of success making the Mastercard ecosystem safer and more secure for our customers. The ideal candidate will be passionate about Financial Crime and have a strong background in AML, PEP & Sanctions. This role is key to the success of Mastercard in the AML ecosystem. Role Responsibilities involve a mix of strategic advice and hands-on implementation: • Risk Assessment: Conducting comprehensive, enterprise-wide AML and sanctions risk assessments to identify vulnerabilities and assess existing controls. • Policy and Procedure Development: Designing, reviewing, enhancing, and developing AML program governance, including tailored policies, procedures, and risk appetite statements aligned requirements • Compliance Monitoring and Testing: Developing and executing independent testing and audit procedures to evaluate the effectiveness of AML controls and identify compliance gaps. • Investigations and Due Diligence: Assisting with transaction monitoring, performing "lookbacks" (retrospective reviews), conducting customer due diligence (CDD) and enhanced due diligence (EDD) for high-risk clients, and making recommendations on suspicious activity reporting (SARs). • Remediation and Regulatory Response: Advising clients on resolving deficiencies, managing remediation plans • System Implementation and Optimization: Assisting in the selection, implementation, tuning, and validation of AML technology systems and software to improve efficiency and accuracy. • Stakeholder Collaboration: Serving as a subject matter expert (SME), liaising with senior management, global product, legal, operations, and technology teams to implement effective compliance solutions. All About You • Years related business experience in AML advisory, product management, product development, business development in financial services and/or bankcard industry preferred. • Excellent oral and written communication, negotiation, project management and matrix-management skills are critical • Ability to interact and coordinate effectively with internal and external business partners • Ability to effectively direct and manage multiple functions • Ability to initiate and effectively lead change efforts • Business Acumen with the ability to provide solutions that drive positive results to the customer and Mastercard • Self-motivated with a proven track record of delivering success while operating within a team environment • Embody the MA leadership principles: demonstrate courage & resolution; encourage teamwork, integration, & inclusion; execute the global strategy; inspire & empower; operate with a sense of urgency Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organi …

As a Finance Advisor You will be responsible for: Leading financial planning cycle (3-year plan, budgeting, forecasting) and defining KPIs to track organizational performance. Analyzing business performance, identifying improvement opportunities, and driving profitability, cash flow, and corrective action plans. Acting as a strategic finance advisor to leadership, guiding projects, initiatives, and business decision-making. Leading and implementing long-term projects (1–2 years) with measurable operational and financial impact. Driving EBITDA improvement across underperforming units by identifying root causes and executing recovery plans. Serving as finance focal point for ADNOC Drilling, managing stakeholder relationships and ensuring strong customer engagement. Strengthening cash flow and collections by reducing backlog, resolving billing issues, and maintaining regular financial reconciliation with the customer. Overseeing financial governance including audits, month-end closing, forecasts, reporting, and executive presentations. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. jobs.title: Sales Manager
jobs.description: Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Join our global team to grow your expertise and develop innovative solutions that enable a prosperous and more sustainable society. About the role The Sales Manager is responsible for providing technical expertise and support throughout the sales process, from initial customer engagement to post-sale implementation. This role involves analyzing customer requirements, preparing technical proposals, and delivering product demonstrations. The Sales Manager ensures that solutions are technically sound and aligned with customer needs. Responsible for developing and executing sales strategies driving order intake, profitability, and market share growth for industrial equipment and solutions. The role focuses on key accounts, EPCs, end users, and government-related industrial projects , while ensuring strong internal coordination with global factories, service centers, and regional business units. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales & Business Development Own and drive sales performance across GCC countries , UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, and Bahrain. Develop and execute country-level and regional sales plans aligned with business unit strategy . Lead the full sales cycle : opportunity identification, RFQ/tender review, commercial strategy, negotiation, and order booking. Build and maintain strong relationships with key end users, EPCs, consultants, and government entities . 2. Key Account & Stakeholder Management Act as the primary commercial contact for assigned strategic accounts across the GCC. Manage sensitive customer matters such as pricing discussions, contractual clarifications, delivery issues, and escalations in a professional and diplomatic manner. Ensure alignment with local agents and partners , while complying with company governance and agency policies. 3. Tendering & Commercial Governance Coordinate complex tender and proposal activities , ensuring close collaboration with global tendering teams, factories, and engineering. Ensure correct commercial positioning , margins, terms & conditions, and risk assessment for each opportunity. Ensure accurate order booking , including correct business unit ownership, SAP application codes, and P&L attribution. Support intercompany coordination where projects involve multiple legal entities or regions. 4. Internal Coordination & Execution Act as a bridge between customers and internal teams : engineering, project management, supply chain, factories, and service units. Follow up on order confirmations, manufacturing progress, logistics, and delivery milestones to ensure customer satisfaction. Drive internal alignment and proactively escalate risks that could impact delivery, cost, or customer relationship. 5. Sales Reporting & Market Intelligence Maintain accurate CRM pipeline, forecasts, and sales reports . Provide regular insights on market trends, competitor activities, and customer investment plans across the GCC. Support budgeting, forecasting, and strategic planning processes.. What we offer you As per local requirements Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

jobs.title: Manager, SME Product & Partnership Management
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Manager, SME Product & Partnership Management Manager, SME Product & Partnership management role will support the Head of SME Product Development EEMEA, to lead the development & management of SME solutions and partnerships in EEMEA . These solutions and partnerships will support Issuers and acquirers as well as the end SME user. They may also support additional distribution partners like the governments, fintechs, and digital players. This cross-functional position will be interfacing with global product, regional sales teams, account management, business development, and external partners to ensure Mastercard maintains and grows its leadership in the SME segment. jobs.title: Senior Contracts Manager
jobs.description: Job Description Senior Contracts Manager The Senior Contracts Manager will be responsible to manage SP&S deals from a contracting and commercial perspective throughout the deal lifecycle, ensuring compliance with contract commitments and commercial risk assessment during all phases. In this role they negotiate contract terms and conditions, provide commercial and contractual leadership and direction throughout the entire deal life-cycle and develop practical and innovative ways to identify & mitigate contract risk. Partner the best The Senior Contracts Manager will be responsible for: Developing & negotiating detailed contractual agreements with customers, and leading cross-functional teams in the contract negotiation, contributing to define the overall deal strategy. Supporting the bid teams in proposal development, risk management (including deal risk and governance adherence - deal reviews, etc.), scope definition, pricing and incentives, customer communications and issue resolution. Managing all commercial and contractual related aspects of a project during the execution phase, including but not limited to commercial arrangements, Variation Orders, contract negotiation, amendments, adherence to contract requirements, etc. Ensuring compliance with the project specific contractual administration requirements (establish and maintain the project variation control system, managing the invoicing process, etc..). Providing advice, assistance and guidance to management, project managers, engineering, sourcing and other functions on project specific contractual matters. Ensuring commercial risks are identified and managed across the project scopes of work by controlling the overall project risk and opportunity review and reporting process. Managing negotiations in line with contract requirements, including supporting the formulation and negotiation of project contractual claims and counter claims. Fuel your passion Have a Bachelor’s Degree in Law/Business or related experience. Have detailed knowledge of energy contracts, terms and conditions and negotiation techniques and contracts negotiation. Strong commercial leadership skills with proven capability of working in a matrix environment and of leading by influence. Understanding of key contractual and commercial risks and areas for opportunity and ability to lead project risk review process and assessments. Negotiating Skills: To secure favorable outcomes, having the flexibility to understand and negotiate alternative contractual and commercial positions with customers (subject to approval). Strong commercial acumen and business understanding Excellent oral and written communication skills. Effective Team player. Be willing to travel. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. We work in hybrid model : 4 days from office and 1 day from home (remote) Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: · Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities · Comprehensive private medical care options · Safety net of life insurance and disability programs · Tailored financial programs · Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Sales Advisor - Proposals

jobs.title: Mechanical Technician

Follow maintenance schedules and safety protocols. Document work performed and report issues. Skills & Qualifications May require basic vocational training (or equivalent work experience) and the ability to carry out assigned tasks after initial instruction or demonstration. Mechanical aptitude and hands-on skills. Ability to read technical diagrams. Attention to detail and safety. Willingness to learn and follow instructions. What we offer you As per local requirements Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

jobs.title: Testing & Commissioning Engineer - Freight Facilities
jobs.description: Testing & Commissioning Engineer role will support Freight Facilities as part of a transformative rail infrastructure project. The scope includes formation preparation, track installation, freight yard development, and systems integration, ensuring seamless operations and world class standards. Candidates must have prior experience working on similar rail, freight facilities, or major infrastructure projects. Plan, coordinate, and supervise testing and commissioning activities for civil and infrastructure projects. Ensure all testing and commissioning works are carried out in accordance with project specifications, approved procedures, and authority requirements. Prepare and review testing and commissioning plans, procedures, checklists, and method statements. Coordinate with contractors, consultants, subcontractors, and authorities during testing and commissioning activities. Monitor testing activities for utilities networks including water supply, sewerage, stormwater, irrigation, and associated civil infrastructure. Witness and verify hydrostatic testing, pressure testing, leakage testing, gravity line testing, compaction testing, and other required tests. Ensure all materials, equipment, and installations are tested and commissioned as per approved standards and manufacturer recommendations. Review shop drawings, IFC drawings, specifications, and technical submittals before commencement of testing activities. Verify readiness of systems and completion of pre-commissioning requirements before final commissioning. Coordinate interface activities between civil, mechanical, electrical, and utility systems during commissioning stages. Ensure testing equipment and instruments are calibrated and valid prior to testing activities. Prepare and maintain testing records, commissioning reports, inspection reports, and handover documentation. Identify defects, non-conformities, and incomplete works during testing and ensure corrective actions are implemented. Raise NCRs and site observations for non-compliant works where required. Monitor contractor performance and ensure testing activities are completed within project schedules. Coordinate with utility authorities for inspections, approvals, and final connections. Attend testing, commissioning, and technical coordination meetings with project stakeholders. Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and quality requirements during all testing activities. Support project handover processes by ensuring all testing and commissioning documentation is complete and approved. Verify as-built drawings and O&M manuals before project closeout. Ensure compliance with requirements of authorities. Assist the Resident Engineer and Project Management team in resolving technical and commissioning-related issues. Monitor final snagging and ensure closure of all testing and commissioning comments before project handover.   Minimum Requirements:  The candidate would need to have prior experience in similar projects.  Middle East experience would be advantageous  Minimum of 5+ years of relevant site supervision experience  Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant Engineering Field + experience on Railway projects.

jobs.title: Land Surveyor
jobs.description: Job Summary AECOM is seeking an experienced Land Surveyor major project located in Dubai. Job Duties Perform survey work under the direction and guidance of the Senior Highway Surveyor. Establish primary horizontal and vertical control points including reference ties for re-establishing the points in the field Record all measurements, rod readings and calculations neatly and accurately in field books as the work progresses. Coordinate with the Contractor’s surveyor on work priorities and schedules. Check all work by completing traverse closures and bench circuits. Report discrepancies or technical problems to the Assistant Resident Engineer. Perform joint measurements of the completed work or payment Maintain equipment in proper condition and perform frequent calibration checks. Specific Skills Required Strong Construction supervision experiences on expressways projects. Well experienced in the construction of interchanges / intersections. Ability to utilize modern computer software and survey equipment Qualification Relevant university or college qualification (Min 7 Years of Diploma in Civil or Survey) RTA/ DM  Approval is preferred Experience Work experience of not less than 10 years in construction and/or supervision Must also have knowledge of existing networks in the Emirate of Dubai

jobs.title: Engineer - Interface - Freight Facilities
jobs.description: We are seeking an Engineer - Interface to coordinate and control interfaces between civil works and multidisciplinary packages on a complex rail and infrastructure program. You will drive resolution of design and construction interfaces across structural, architectural, utilities, MEP, rail systems, roads, and wider infrastructure—ensuring safe, compliant, and constructible outcomes aligned to schedule and quality. We value discretion and professional conduct in operational environments. Key Responsibilities Coordinate and manage interfaces between civil works and Structural, Architectural, Utilities, MEP, Rail Systems, Roads, and Infrastructure disciplines. Review design drawings across packages; identify interface conflicts or gaps and drive timely resolution. Ensure smooth coordination between contractor, consultant, client, and subcontractors; establish clear communication pathways Monitor interface issues and maintain interface registers, trackers, and action logs with owners and due dates. Organize and lead interface/coordination meetings; issue minutes, decisions, and follow-up actions. Review RFIs, technical queries, and design changes related to interface matters; escalate risks and secure approvals as required. Ensure all interfacing works comply with project specifications, standards, and approved drawings. Coordinate construction sequencing, access requirements, and work windows between multiple contractors/packages. Identify potential clashes and assist in resolving technical conflicts before execution (design and construction phases). Support design coordination and constructability reviews, including temporary works and staging plans. Liaise with authorities and utility agencies to align on interface requirements, permits, and approvals. Track progress of interfacing activities and align dependencies with the integrated project schedule. Coordinate handover requirements and interface deliverables between contractors and across project phases. Assist in reviewing shop drawings, method statements, and material submissions for interface compliance. Maintain auditable records of interface communications, approvals, and action item closure. Ensure temporary works, diversions, and logistics do not adversely impact adjacent contractors or systems. Coordinate civil interfaces spanning Roads, Bridges, Tunnels, Stations, Drainage, Utilities, and Landscaping. Support risk identification, mitigation planning, and contingency for interface-related issues. Participate in site inspections to verify interface compliance during execution; close observations promptly. Ensure all stakeholders are informed of design revisions and construction changes impacting interfaces. Coordinate testing and commissioning interfaces between civil and other systems; verify pre-requisites and dependencies. Assist the Project Manager and Resident Engineer in resolving interface-related delays and conflicts. Monitor close-out and handover activities to confirm interface requirements are completed and accepted. Prepare interface progress reports and status updates for management and client review. • Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or a closely related engineering discipline. • Proven experience coordinating interfaces on large, multidisciplinary infrastructure programs (e.g., rail, roads, utilities). • Experience working on freight facilities

jobs.title: Architectural Inspector
jobs.description: We are looking for an Inspector - Architectural role for our Dubai site office. Reporting to the Discipline Engineers, the Inspector is responsible for conducting site inspections, surveillance, and testing of construction activities to ensure compliance with construction documents and relevant standards. Working closely with their assigned engineer, the Inspector monitors and records all contractor activities on-site to support quality assurance and project compliance.· Monitoring and ensuring that all architectural engineering aspects of the project are carried out as per inspection plan/drawings/codes and specifications. Liaising with the Senior Architectural  Engineer and other Engineers and Managers to provide support and guidance as required Review and monitor variations to the work to mitigate time and cost impacts for any required changes Monitor the review of the contractor’s documents/shop drawings to flag and rectify any issues which may affect the stability or function of any architectural components or systems. Monitor contractor’s compliance with approved Quality Control Plan. Monitor the project related protocol set up for Inspection of works by the inspection teams. Monitor safety aspects when dealing with architectural  installations Provide reports on the progress of the architectural aspects of the project. Assist in expediting of NOCs from relevant authorities if they appear to be stalled or in delay Maintain full site records, including daily site diaries in an approved format, and formal monthly progress photography. Expedition of approvals from the Client if it appears to be stalled or delaying the project Liaising with the management of the various contractors to expedite submissions and approvals process. Perform and or evaluate tests confirming quality requirements, architectural finishes and building components by conducting thorough site inspection for all architectural works as per approved shop drawings, specifications and method statement. Field checks placement, location and quality of architectural finishes, building structure components. Monitor contractor’s compliance with approved Quality Control Plan. Coordinating with the team on site to ensure that contractual obligations are met. Perform Architectural inspections and material inspections. Issue NCRs to contractors and assist in taking corrective actions. Ensure that all inspection reports are accurate and in compliance with the requirements. Ensure that all execution and inspection are carried out as per inspection plan/drawings/codes and specifications. Ensure compliance of architectural works to design requirements, specifications and project plan. Preparing daily logs and reports. Maintain records of all submittals and testing inspections made, tests performed, work executed, etc. Performing all work in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Assisting with the inspection of completed works. Perform all other duties given by direct manager. Monitoring and reporting on HSE related matters and participating in HSE training and meetings Experience in architectural-type projects required.   Bachelor's degree or a diploma in architecture is required. Candidates must have experience in Construction Supervision with a Consultancy with a minimum of 8 years of overall experience (minimum 5 years in the UAE)

jobs.title: Senior Architect
jobs.description: Job Summary: A Senior Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. Key Responsibilities: Site Supervision: Oversee the construction site to ensure that the architectural design is implemented correctly and according to the plans. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of materials and workmanship to ensure compliance with design specifications and standards. Coordination: Collaborate with contractors, engineers, and other professionals to resolve any design or construction issues. Compliance: Ensure that all construction activities comply with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental guidelines. Problem-Solving: Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise on-site, making adjustments to plans as necessary. Reporting: Prepare and present progress reports to clients and stakeholders, highlighting any deviations from the plan and proposed solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of site activities, including daily logs, inspection reports, and change orders. Client Interaction: Liaise with clients to understand their needs and ensure their satisfaction with the project. Minimum 10 years demonstrable experience in the all aspects of supervision and project administration of the Architectural works related to large-scale projects within Middle East in the consulting industry. Bachelor's Degree in Architectural Engineering or equivalent. Membership of a recognized institution (i.e. PE/Peng, CEng Etc.) would be preferred. LEED GA or AP certifications would be a plus

jobs.title: Graduate Data Scientist إماراتيين (خلاصة القيد)
jobs.description: This opportunity is part of AECOM’s Fursa Program and open exclusively to UAE Nationals holding a valid family book. The candidate will play a pivotal role in leveraging digital tools and technologies to enhance project efficiency, accuracy, and data-driven decision-making. Enhance and support cost planning, cost control, and value engineering processes using digital platforms. Maintain and update databases, ensuring accurate and up-to-date information for project teams. Generate information rich reports and dashboards using data visualization tools. Data Management & Analysis: Collect, organize, and analyze project data to provide actionable insights for cost management. Develop and maintain digital templates, workflows, and automation scripts to improve efficiency. Ensure data integrity and compliance with company standards and protocols. Technology Implementation: Provide training and technical support to team members on digital tools and processes. Stay updated on emerging technologies and recommend innovative solutions to enhance project delivery. Support cross-functional teams in adopting digital practices and fostering a culture of innovation. UAE Nationals with a family book. A degree or diploma in a relevant field such as Quantity Surveying, Digital Engineering, Software Engineering, Computer Science, Data Analytics and the like An understanding of common construction industry digital tools like BIM authoring and management software (e.g., Revit, Navisworks, ACC, Solibri, Aconex), CostX, or similar platforms. Advanced skills in Python, SQL, data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI), and other relevant software. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and teamwork abilities. Experience with programming and developing automation tools with Dynamo, Python, VBA or similar scripting languages. The ideal candidate would have experience working in teams to deliver complex projects with multiple stakeholders. Effective communicate, including speaking to groups and providing training sessions from time to time would be required. Well established organization and time management skills Confident to engage and positively challenge with other team members across disciplines and of different seniorities Be inclusive and supportive and contribute to a positive and productive team environment

jobs.title: Civil Inspector - (rails/ tracks)
jobs.description: We're looking for a detail-oriented Civil Inspector for Rails/Tracks to join our freight facilities operations team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for conducting comprehensive inspections of railway tracks and rail infrastructure at our freight facilities, ensuring compliance with safety standards and operational excellence. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a commitment to maintaining the highest standards of safety and quality across all freight facility operations. Inspect rail and track construction activities to ensure compliance with approved drawings, specifications, project requirements, and quality standards. Monitor installation of rail tracks, sleepers, ballast, fastening systems, and related civil works. Verify track alignment, levels, gauge, cant, and geometry as per approved tolerances. Inspect earthworks, subgrade preparation, formation layers, and concrete works related to railway infrastructure. Ensure proper execution of welding works, rail joints, thermite welding, and stress adjustment activities. Conduct inspection of track slab construction, reinforcement, embedded items, and concrete pouring activities where applicable. Review shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and inspection test plans (ITPs). Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects, particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures, including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

jobs.title: Senior Advisor - Safety, Health & Environment
jobs.description: The Safety Engineer for Sewerage Networks (Micro tunneling) holds a critical role in ensuring the utmost safety of personnel, equipment, and the environment throughout the lifecycle of sewerage system projects. This position demands unwavering commitment to maintaining the highest standards of health and safety in all aspects of sewerage network construction. Conduct comprehensive and meticulous risk assessments for sewerage network construction projects, with particular emphasis on identifying and evaluating potential hazards related to environmental factors, equipment operation, material handling, and work processes. Critically review and enhance risk mitigation strategies to effectively minimize the likelihood of accidents and health risks, ensuring full compliance with all relevant safety regulations and industry best practices. Perform thorough examinations of plans and specifications for sewer and stormwater networks, meticulously identifying potential hazards and risks that may compromise safety or operational integrity. Develop and oversee robust emergency response plans to address critical incidents such as chemical spills, equipment failures, or accidents, ensuring swift and effective action in crisis situations. Establish and maintain essential coordination with relevant authorities for emergency services, facilitating immediate and appropriate responses when required. Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge and strict adherence to all safety and engineering standards as stipulated in DM and RTA manuals, ensuring full compliance across all project phases. Exhibit expertise in the preparation and management of traffic diversion schemes at work zones, prioritizing public safety and minimizing disruption to local infrastructure. Bachelor's degree or higher in engineering, preferably in civil, environmental or health and safety engineering Mandatory 3-5 years of experience in UAE preferred

jobs.title: Quantity Surveyor
jobs.description: Conduct surveys to ascertain the locations of natural features and human-made structures on the earth's surface, underground, and underwater using electronic distance-measuring equipment and surveying instruments. Analyze survey data, research legal records, and determine boundary line locations based on measurement of distances, directions, angles, and elevation points. Operate land-information computer systems to store data, perform inquiries, and generate plots and reports. Record survey results, verify data accuracy, and prepare comprehensive maps and reports for stakeholders. Work independently on diverse surveying projects with minimal supervision on routine assignments. Bachelor's Degree in Quantity Surveying, Engineering, or related Construction field Chartered RICS Membership (MRICS) or working towards APC Minimum 5 years post-graduate experience, including 2-3 years in the Middle East

jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Strong quantitative and qualitative thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent communication and presentation skills Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment Strong cross-functional leadership skills to align product, analytics, and operations teams. Technical Knowledge General fluency in the marketing technology ecosystem (APIs, integrations, marketing, CRM, customer engagement, loyalty, analytics, customer data tools) High degree of comfort working with analytics dashboards and translating insights into action Attributes Empathy and a strong ability to understand customer goals and needs Strategic and critical thinker with a proactive mindset Preferred Qualifications Experience with loyalty program design and optimization. jobs.title: Digital Technology Senior Specialist - Network Operations Engineering
jobs.description: Responsibilities, authorities and accountabilities In this role, you will: Hands-on Network Engineer with understanding and passion for technology, works cross functionally to solve and has a passion for internal and external customer success Ability to navigate and solve in a multi-tenant, complex Datacenter Network environment Solution oriented and obsessed with quality outcomes Drives solutions that improve resiliency and reliability across the enterprise with intense focus on capability and contemporized designs and solutions Has a robust understanding of the environment including *Unix, Windows, and storage fabrics Interfaces with Engineering, Security, and Network Architecture to align implementations and drives outcomes through the eyes of customer success Builds and manages the DC LAN environments Subject matter expert in processes and methodologies with ability to adapt and improvise in various situations. Expert in navigating through ambiguity and prioritizing conflicting asks. jobs.title: Senior Advisor - Safety, Health & Environment
jobs.description: Job Summary we are seeking a highly experienced and detail-oriented Senior Advisor - Safety, Health and Environment to join our team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. In this critical role, you will lead our efforts to ensure compliance with SH&E regulations, develop and implement comprehensive safety programs, and foster a culture of safety and environmental responsibility across our organization. Key Job Duties: Serve as the primary point of contact for all SH&E-related matters, providing expert guidance to management and staff. Develop, implement, and oversee comprehensive SH&E strategies and programs aligned with organizational goals and regulatory requirements. Conduct regular risk assessments and safety audits to identify potential hazards and areas for improvement. Lead investigations into accidents, injuries, and near-misses, implementing corrective actions to prevent recurrence. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate SH&E best practices into all aspects of operations. Supervise and mentor junior SH&E staff, fostering their professional development and ensuring high-quality performance. Stay updated on UAE and international SH&E regulations, standards, and best practices, ensuring organizational compliance. Develop and deliver SH&E training programs to enhance awareness and competency across the organization. Prepare and present detailed SH&E reports and recommendations to senior management. Manage emergency response planning and crisis management initiatives. Liaise with regulatory bodies and represent the organization in SH&E-related matters. Bachelor's degree in Occupational Safety, Environmental Science, or a related field. NEBOSH Diploma or equivalent certification (required for candidates with Engineering or related degrees). Minimum of 10 years of experience in a relevant SH&E field, with a focus on supervision and leadership. Experience in roads and infrastructure projects.  Proven track record in developing, implementing, and managing effective safety and health programs. In-depth knowledge of UAE and international SH&E legislative and regulatory obligations. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with excellent risk assessment and mitigation skills. Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence at all levels of the organization. Proficiency in using relevant software and tools for SH&E management and reporting. Demonstrated experience in conducting inspections, reporting issues, and following up on corrective actions. Familiarity with international SH&E standards (e.g., ISO 45001, ISO 14001). Strong leadership and supervisory skills, with the ability to mentor and develop team members. Knowledge of local UAE safety regulations and standards. Willingness to travel within the UAE as required for site inspections and audits.

jobs.title: Account Manager II
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

About the Team
Careem Express is a vertical within the Careem ecosystem – a startup within a startup. We are responsible for fulfillment and last mile delivery for hundreds of merchants in a variety of industries, including e-commerce, food, grocery, and other areas. We are a small and passionate team that is building the next big vertical for Careem.senior-commercial-lead is at the core of Careem Express. They are our face to clients, and we rely on them to build a strong relationship with enterprises and important clients & build on our strong pipeline targeting non-food clients. They are responsible for growing existing Tier 1 clients and bringing in demand in a sustainable way.
What You’ll Do
  • Business development - Acquire new accounts across UAE (prospecting, targeting, pitching, contract negotiations, go-live)
  • Build and manage relationships with existing tier 1 clients
  • Achieve monthly Revenue targets through existing and new B2B clients
  • Commercial understanding and handling negotiations with clients
  • Coordinate with operations / logistics team internally for support to make sure that the pitches are made at the right commercials
  • Coordinate with Legal internally for contract closures
What You’ll Need
  • 2-3 years of Account Management/Sales experience from non food B2B, Ecommerce or on-demand logistics industry
  • High performer – track record of generating revenue for businesses
  • Business development mindset coupled with Operational d …

Support training execution as required Fuel your passion! To be successful in this role you will have: Bachelor's degree from an accredited university or college. Minimum of 5 years of Baker Hughes wireline experience. Recognized as an experienced coach/mentor for field personnel, with the ability to transfer operational know-how through structured training and on-the-job coaching. Ability to serve as an SME and competency assessor in at least one wireline service, contributing to CMS/CMP development and competency sign-off processes. Strong oral and written communication skills. Strong interpersonal and leadership skills. Ability to influence others and lead small teams. Ability to lead initiatives of moderate scope and impact. Ability to coordinate several projects simultaneously. Effective problem identification and solution skills. Work in a way that works for you. We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us …
