Provide input and support for construction layout, staking, and setting out for roads, pavements, curbs, drainage, and utilities. Monitor and verify contractor work alignment, levels, and grading against design drawings. Coordinate with the contractor's surveyor and Resident Engineer on work priorities, measurements, and discrepancies. Maintain survey equipment in proper working condition and perform frequent calibration checks. Assist in preparing as-built drawings and maintaining documentation of survey records, reports, and maps for project reporting. Utilize modern computer software, GPS, total stations, and other survey equipment for accurate measurements. Ensure compliance with safety procedures during all survey operations. Bachelor's Degree/Diploma in relevant Engineering field (i.e. Civil, Geodetic or related field.) 7 -10 years of Experience as Land surveyor is preferable.  Only UAE candidates are eleigible to apply

jobs.title: Contracts Administrator - Rail
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Contracts Administrator to support the commercial and contract management functions on major rail and infrastructure projects. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring effective contract administration, contractual compliance, and accurate documentation throughout the project lifecycle. This position requires strong knowledge of contract management principles, including experience administering contracts under FIDIC forms of contract, within large-scale design and construction environments. Key Requirements 5–10 years’ experience in contract administration within major rail and/or infrastructure projects Experience working on rail, metro, transportation, or other large-scale infrastructure developments is preferred Strong understanding of contract administration principles, commercial processes, and document control procedures Working knowledge and practical experience with FIDIC contracts   Experience supporting the management of variations, claims, contractual notices, and change control processes Strong organisational, analytical, and communication skills with excellent attention to detail Ability to coordinate effectively with multidisciplinary teams, consultants, contractors, and stakeholders Experience on complex, high-value infrastructure projects is highly desirable Middle East experience, particularly within the UAE, will be considered an advantage Key Responsibilities Support the administration and management of project contracts in accordance with contractual requirements and internal governance procedures Assist in the interpretation and application of FIDIC contract provisions and contractual obligations Maintain accurate and up-to-date contract records, correspondence, registers, and documentation Assist in the preparation, review, and processing of variations, claims, extension of time submissions, and contractual notices Monitor contractual deliverables, obligations, and key milestones to ensure compliance by all parties Coordinate with commercial, legal, engineering, and project delivery teams on contract-related matters Support the preparation of commercial reports, risk assessments, and contract status updates for management review Identify potential contractual risks and support mitigation strategies Ensure adherence to project procedures, contractual processes, and corporate governance requirements Support audits and ensure proper filing and traceability of contractual documentation throughout the project lifecycle Bachelor’s Degree in Quantity Surveying

jobs.title: Senior Engineer - Mechanical
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: AECOM is seeking a highly skilled and experienced  Senior Mechanical Engineer  to join our  Buildings & Places (B&P) Construction Supervision  team. This role involves overseeing mechanical engineering aspects of construction projects, ensuring compliance with design specifications, quality standards, and project timelines. The ideal candidate will bring technical expertise, leadership capabilities, and a commitment to delivering excellence in the built environment. Construction Supervision & Quality Assurance Oversee the installation, testing, and commissioning of mechanical systems, including HVAC, plumbing, fire protection, and other building services. Independently perform walk-downs and inspections of installed commodities to ensure conformance with specifications and design. Ensure all mechanical works comply with project specifications, codes, standards, and safety regulations. Conduct regular site inspections to monitor progress and address technical issues. Technical Leadership Provide technical guidance to project teams, contractors, and stakeholders. Review and approve mechanical design drawings, shop drawings, and material submittals. Display knowledge and abilities to evaluate, select, and apply standard engineering techniques, procedures, and criteria to meet basic engineering and construction specifications. May provide technical direction to drafters, technicians, and surveyors. Project Coordination Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, including architects, civil engineers, and electrical engineers, to ensure seamless integration of mechanical systems. Coordinate with contractors and subcontractors to ensure timely execution of mechanical works. Documentation & Reporting Prepare and maintain detailed site reports, inspection records, and progress updates. Perform quantity surveys for tracking and reporting. Ensure accurate documentation of as-built drawings and project handover deliverables. Compliance & Risk Management Ensure compliance with local building codes, environmental regulations, and health and safety standards. Identify potential risks and implement mitigation strategies to minimize project delays or cost overruns. Professional Judgment Display sound professional judgment on details of work, ensuring engineering solutions align with project goals and standards. Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field (Master’s degree preferred). Minimum of 10 years of experience in mechanical engineering, with at least 5 years in construction supervision for B&P projects. Proficiency in mechanical design software (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit MEP, BIM tools). Strong understanding of mechanical systems, building codes, and construction methodologies. Knowledge of international standards such as ASHRAE, NFPA, and LEED is a plus. Excellent leadership and team management abilities. Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills. Effective communication and interpersonal skills to liaise with clients, contractors, and team members.

jobs.title: Inspector - Material
jobs.description: Inspect site conditions prior to the start of construction activities to verify the compatibility of the site with the approved design and permanent works. • Check for obstructions and document existing site conditions through photographs to prevent potential damage to existing utilities, structures, and landscaping. • Review and verify materials delivered to the site for conformance with approved samples, specifications, and equipment lists; report any non-conformances to the Resident Engineer (RE). • Ensure that all materials are properly stored and protected at the site to maintain quality and prevent damage or contamination. • Inspect construction of both temporary and permanent works to confirm compliance with contract documents, approved shop drawings, and project specifications. • Prepare and maintain accurate daily inspection logs, test reports, and site records in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. • Monitor site safety practices and ensure that all works are carried out following approved safety procedures; report unsafe practices or incidents to the RE immediately. • Issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) and Site Observation Reports (SORs) for any deviation from approved materials, specifications, or procedures, and follow up on corrective actions. • Assist in the inspection of completed works, prepare punch lists, and monitor testing, commissioning, and handover activities to ensure quality compliance. • Review and provide recommendations on Contractors’ Requests for Information (RFIs) related to civil materials and testing requirements. • Evaluate requests for material variances or substitutions and provide technical comments and recommendations to the RE for approval. • Read and interpret working drawings and related specifications, enforce compliance on-site, and prepare inspection and material testing reports. • Coordinate with third-party testing laboratories and ensure all material tests are performed as per project specifications and standards. • Ensure that all material approvals, test results, NCRs, and inspection records are properly documented and maintained for monthly reporting and project close-out. • Coordinate with relevant authorities (e.g., Dubai Municipality, RTA, DEWA) for material approvals, testing, and compliance verification.   Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in Civil Engineering or a related discipline from an accredited institution. Minimum of 7+ years of professional experience working as a Material Inspector, with a strong focus on large-scale infrastructure projects. Candidates must have proven experience in: Rail and Metro projects (such as UAE Metro, Etihad Rail, or similar international rail systems) Freight rail or track infrastructure projects , including track works, ballast installation, and rail system components Demonstrated in-depth knowledge of railway construction materials , including but not limited to rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, fastening systems, concrete, and structural steel. Solid understanding of track systems and rail infrastructure , including material specifications, installation practices, and quality control measures specific to rail environments. Hands-on experience in material inspection, testing procedures, and quality assurance/quality control (QA/QC) in compliance with international standards such as ASTM, BS, EN, and project-specific requirements. Proven track record of working on major infrastructure projects within the UAE or GCC region , with familiarity in local authority standards (e.g., RTA, DM, or Etihad Rail standards) considered a strong advantage. Ability to effectively interpret technical specifications, drawings, and material submittals , ensuring all materials meet required standards before and during project execution.

jobs.title: Planning Engineer
jobs.description: Job Summary AECOM is seeking an experienced Planning Engineer for a major Roads and Bridges project located in Dubai. This position will be site based in charge of a package (project) responsible for providing planning and scheduling support and assistance to Project Managers and other Project team members. Key Responsibilities • Works with the company financial and cornerstone systems and processes.   • Applies financial terminology, measures, financial data analysis concepts and principles in assignments. • Recognizes processes/project actions that may lead to project financial impact. • Develops and maintains WBS for small projects.  • Assists in the use of drawings and specifications in the establishment of project baseline data.  • Begins basic analysis of cost and schedule variances. • Develops knowledge of accounting principles.    • Assists in assembling data for trending and forecasting. • Understands progress/performance data and associated concepts and principles. Participates in the collection of progress data and review of performance trends. • Familiar with earned value, scope control and change management concepts, principles and methodologies.  • Collects data and produces an initial analysis on manpower, labor hour, and labor cost requirements versus budget limitations.  • Collects and analyzes data for tracking actual cost to funding limitations.  • Provides support to tracking purchase order and subcontract commitments and expenditures. • Familiar with the concepts and principles of project revenue and cost. • Exposure to basic scheduling and cost baseline fundamentals, concepts and principles. • Introduced to performance management and cost/schedule integration concepts and principles. • Develops knowledge of scope control and change management. • Supports project reporting process as required.   • May participate in project cost review meetings. Minimum Qualification A Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or Construction Management. Experience Requirements Minimum of 10+ years’ experience in the Planning / Project Controls field is required. Applicants would ideally have worked on major programs in  transport. Advanced knowledge of ORACLE Primavera P6 Project Management Software. Ability to develop, monitor, and control schedules. Hands-on working knowledge of WBS coding structure, cost /schedule variance analysis, Earned Value Management (EVMS), project trend, contract change and claims. Working knowledge of Time Impact Analysis techniques.

jobs.title: Manager, Product Management Marketing Digital Innovation & Marketing AI Champion
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential. Title and Summary Manager, Product Management Marketing Digital Innovation & Marketing AI Champion Overview As Marketing Services (MS), the marketing strategy & execution arm of Mastercard, we aim to focus on differentiated scalable and digital offerings that leverages our unique data and marketing technology to accelerate campaign performance and associated outcomes. Our vision is to become the partner of choice for Issuers and Merchants leveraging our data-driven marketing performance products to drive our client’s most profitable customer engagements and ultimately Mastercard preference with consumers. Through the transformation of the youngest and fastest growing MS region, EEMEA is seeking an experienced Marketing Services Product Manager. The candidates’ superior understanding of the card portfolio growth drivers and important KPIs to track blended with experience in targeted, data-driven marketing campaigns will deliver better results for clients and create their reliance on MS. We are seeking a high-caliber marketing professional with deep expertise in acquisition and engagement strategies, strong grounding in digital and data-driven marketing, and proven experience in driving innovation using AI and emerging technologies. This individual will play a pivotal role in elevating Mastercard’s value proposition across both traditional and non-traditional segments. Beyond financial institutions, the role will focus on unlocking new partnership opportunities with merchants, telcos, government entities, and broader ecosystem players. Role & Responsibilities - Champion a culture of innovation within MS by proactively identifying innovation opportunities, benchmarking global trends, and introducing new methodologies and tools that redefine how Mastercard and its clients approach customer acquisition and engagement. - Grow MS revenue by designing and implementing next-generation, AI-enabled marketing solutions that are scalable across issuers, merchants, telcos, governments, and emerging ecosystem partners. These solutions will integrate Mastercard’s data assets, advanced analytics, and marketing technology to deliver end-to-end acquisition, engagement, and portfolio growth programs that materially differentiate Mastercard in both traditional and new segments. - Leverage AI, machine learning, and emerging marketing technologies to design and deliver innovative acquisition and engagement campaigns, working closely with creative agencies, media partners, and technology vendors to develop breakthrough concepts, new use cases, and differentiated execution models that elevate marketing performance. - Translate new technology trends into actionable go-to-market strategies, ensuring that Mastercard remains ahead of competitors by operationalizing AI-driven campaign design, real-time optimization, and automated personalization at scale. - Identify and shape new scalable marketing plays for emerging segments (e.g., small merchants, large retailers, telcos, public-sector programs), adapting MS capabilities to fit their unique acquisition and engagement needs. - Lead experimentation frameworks and innovation sprints to test new channels, formats, value propositions, and AI tools that can unlock incremental growth for Mastercard clients across both carded and non-carded consumer segments. - Drive end-to-end digital marketing which includes analytical review of media strategies and plans, trouble shoot and work with partners to propose succ …

jobs.title: GaffneyCline Regional Director
jobs.description: GaffneyCline Regional Director Would you like the opportunity to work for oilfield services company? Do you enjoy creating value and growth through new opportunities? Join our Team! We are the leader in well construction and production. Our innovative physical and digital solutions improve efficiency, production and maximize reservoir value. We provide technical and sales solutions for our customers. Partner with the best As the GaffneyCline Regional Director, you will be responsible for: Leading and managing the Region team from a discipline and business perspective Delivering annual revenue plan per MRP in revenue and OI. Managing headcount, revenue and cost in close collaboration and with full goal alignment with the product lines and areas within region. Providing subsurface related advice and input to multi-disciplinary projects, from exploration through discovery and appraisal to field development and production to drive revenue and margin opportunity for Baker Hughes. Developing a clear vision on the business and the strategy of the business in close interaction with business management. Delivering very complex specialized professional work by single-layered or specialized professional work delivered by multi-layered regional or global teams. Gauging workflow and implementing change where needed to manage the efficiency of a multi-layered department and influence team leaders. Reporting on a regular cadence with peers and executive leadership Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: Have a Bachelor's degree from an accredited university or college on subsurface disciplines such as Petroleum Engineering, Reservoir Engineering, Geology or Geoscience. Have a minimum of 15 additional years of experience in Business Operations Have significant experience in a leadership role directly and indirectly managing multi disciplinary teams Have proven experience within and outside the organization Have the ability to work with and influence stakeholders Be fluent in Arabic - knowledge of regional languages is desired Have P&L management experience Have strong oral and written communication skills. Have strong interpersonal and leadership skills. Have demonstrated ability to analyze and resolve problems. Have established project management skills. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: • Working remotely from home or any other work location • Starting early / finishing early • Starting late / finishing late • Working flexible hours - flexing the times you work in the day • Reducing the time you work during the day • Reducing the time you work across the week Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. We prioritize rewarding those who embrace change with a package that reflects how much we value their input. Join us, and you can expect: · Contemporary work-life balance policies and wellbeing activities · Comprehensive private medical care options · Safety net of life insurance and disability programs · Tailored financial programs · Additional elected or voluntary benefits The Baker Hughes internal title for this role is: Engineering Staff Director - Geoservices

jobs.title: Civil Inspector - Utilities
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Civil Inspector - Utilities to join our team in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will conduct comprehensive inspections of utility infrastructure and systems to ensure compliance with local regulations and industry standards. You will demonstrate exceptional organizational skills, technical expertise, and a commitment to maintaining the highest safety and quality standards across utility operations. While the role focuses on civil works for wet utilities (water, drainage, sewer), you will coordinate with dry utilities stakeholders where applicable. Key Responsibilities Conduct routine, scheduled, and complaint-based inspections of utility infrastructure, with primary emphasis on water distribution, stormwater/drainage, and sewerage networks; coordinate with electrical, gas, and telecommunications stakeholders as applicable to interface points. Document inspection findings in detail and prepare comprehensive, accurate inspection reports using established protocols and software systems. Identify code violations, safety hazards, maintenance deficiencies, and areas requiring immediate attention or remediation. Verify compliance with local UAE regulations, industry standards, and established safety protocols during all inspections. Coordinate with maintenance, repair, and operations teams to communicate findings and recommend corrective actions. Maintain organized and up-to-date inspection records, databases, and documentation for audit and reference purposes. Respond promptly to stakeholder complaints and service requests by conducting follow-up inspections and investigations. Provide technical recommendations for system repairs, upgrades, or replacements based on inspection findings. Collaborate with regulatory agencies and stakeholders to ensure adherence to utility service standards. Participate in training programs to stay current with evolving regulations and inspection best practices. Plan and execute inspections in operational environments with restricted access, aligning activities with site safety, permit-to-work requirements, and operational windows typically associated with logistics or freight-adjacent corridors. Handle sensitive information discreetly and maintain confidentiality regarding project scope, operations, and locations. Minimum 5 years of professional experience in utilities inspection, infrastructure assessment, or related field Strong technical knowledge of utility systems including water, electrical, gas, and telecommunications infrastructure Experience working on a freight facility / Rail Experience

jobs.title: Program Manager
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

About the role

As a Program Manager within the Product organisation, you will be the bridge that brings colleagues together to deliver complex, cross-functional technical initiatives — often with tight deadlines and multi-layered dependencies. You will build relationships grounded in trust and integrity across the organisation and with third-party partners, enabling you to break through collaboration barriers, resolve conflicts, and manage risks effectively.

This role sits within the Rides Product team, which leads product strategy and delivery for the Rides vertical to simplify mobility for customers. You will play a critical role ensuring complex programmes land on time, to a high standard, and with full visibility across the organisation — including across third-party integration boundaries.

What you'll do

  • Work closely with engineers, product managers, designers, researchers, data analysts, commercial leaders, and operations teams to conceptualise cross-functional initiatives with layered dependencies and plan their execution
  • Organise and align multiple stakeholders — including third-party partners — to drive timely, accountable execution
  • Develop a solid understanding of the technical architecture to exercise sound judgment on risks, trade-offs, and cross-team dependencies
  • Drive progress transparency through structured communication of status, blockers, and upcoming milestones across all levels of the organisation
  • Lead incident coordination, post-mortems, and closure of action items to build systemic resilience over time
  • Own go-live orchestration for major launches — including rollout gates, rollback triggers, and cross-functional readiness sign-offs
  • Track and flag resourcing risks to support delivery pace and quality
  • Maintain the programme's source of truth: decision logs, dependency registers, and status documentation
  • Contribute to process improvement within the programme scope — identifying friction points and proposing better ways of working
  • Support and contribute to tools and dashboards that surface delivery health, pace, and quality for the Rides vertical
  • Embed AI tooling into core programme workflows — synthesizing meeting intelligence, surfacing dependency risks, and maintaining high-quality stakeholder comms at pace — and actively raise the team's capability to do the same

What you'll need

  • 5+ years of experience as a Program Manager in a fast-paced engineering organisation
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
  • S …

jobs.title: Gas Turbine Inspector
jobs.description: Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Join our global team to grow your expertise and develop innovative solutions that enable a prosperous and more sustainable society. About the role Purpose of the Job: Responsible for the inspection activities required in the SASOS workshop Main Duties involved but not limited to:  Bulk Strip and Detail Strip Inspection of Gas Turbines.  Provision of engine bulk strip and detailed defect reports.  Carry out stage inspections during engine build.  Controlling the workshop Control Order Files and support documentation.  Assisting the Company Quality Assurance representative in the absence of the supervisor.  Ensuring all measuring equipment in the workshop is approved calibrated and certified accordingly IAW the QMS manuals.  Development and revision of workshop procedures as and when required.  Sourcing suitable and effective alternatives to the OEM for required materials/repairs/overhauls.  Shipping and receipt of material to and from SASOS approved vendors.  Assist with in a timely manner the shipping and receipt of material to and from SASOS approved vendors. Progress and expedite all sub-con activity effectively.  Obtaining the most cost effective and minimum turn time for engine material requiring repair/overhaul/replacement.  Issuing of certificates of conformity for all materials being issued to and purchased by customers.  Assisting with shop floor fitting duties as designated by the Charge Hand or Operations Manager.  All other duties as relevant to the post and deemed reasonable by the Company. Education requirements (desirable):  Minimum NC Mechanical Engineering (or equivalent), preferably HNC Mech. Eng. Where formal qualifications are not held, suitable proven experience is essential. Job experience:  Previous experience in a similar role Knowledge requirements (professional, technical, language, safety):  Working knowledge of industrial gas turbines and/or power turbines and associated equipment  Ability to understand and communicate illustrated parts catalogues.  Ability to understand engineering drawings. What we offer you Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

jobs.title: Gas Turbine Fitter
jobs.description: Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Join our global team to grow your expertise and develop innovative solutions that enable a prosperous and more sustainable society. About the role Responsible for the repair and overhaul of industrial gas turbines, power turbines and associated accessories and equipment. Main Tasks: The duties involved include but are not limited to:  Maintaining standards of engineering, appearance, timekeeping, discipline and housekeeping.  The receipt, bulk strip, detail strip, sub assembly and final build of industrial gas turbines and power turbines.  Training of internal employees.  Adherence to and completion of all procedures and documentation controlling the overhaul process.  Accurate recording of all critical fits/clearances.  Highlighting and reporting of critical out-of-tolerance measurements and other non-conforming issues such as damaged or inadequately overhauled components.  Safe and correct use of all equipment and specialised tooling, including engine tooling is stored and maintained correctly and that any defective or missing equipment is reported immediately to supervising staff. Education requirements (desirable):  ITI, SVQ Graduate (or equivalent)., At least 6 years’ experience of overhauling and repairing gas turbines. Job experience:  Previous experience in a similar role would be advantageous. Knowledge requirements (professional, technical, language, safety):  Knowledge of gas turbine repair and refurbishment would be advantageous  Experience working as a senior workshop member in a fabrication, repair or construction environment. Competencies: Good written and verbal communication (English).  Good organisational skills.  Adaptable approach, able to work independently, and function effectively as part of a team.  Strong team player capable of working across various departments and with external stakeholders.  Attention to detail What we offer you TBD Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

jobs.title: Gas Turbine Cleanline Operator
jobs.description: Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Join our global team to grow your expertise and develop innovative solutions that enable a prosperous and more sustainable society. About the role Main Tasks and Responsibilities The duties involved include but are not limited to:  Cleaning of rotating equipment components not limited to aeroderivative gas turbines, pumps, compressors and steam turbines.  Processing of components in various chemical tanks.  Grit and vapour blasting of components.  Hand cleaning of various components.  Operation of ball burnishing machine.  Actively highlighting and assisting in improving quality standards within the department.  Ensure your own health and safety and that of others who may be affected by your acts or omissions  All other duties as relevant to the post and deemed reasonable by the Company. Quality, Health, Safety and Environment Sulzer ASOS is certified to ISO 9001 and 45001. The Company expects all employees to contribute to the effective implementation of its Integrated Management System. For this role, employees are expected to:  Comply with and adhere to the Company’s quality, health & safety and environmental policies and procedures.  Ensure your own health and safety and that of others who may be affected by your acts or omissions.  Proactively engage and support initiatives designed to promote a culture of safe working, quality, environmental protection and continual improvement.  Co-operate with the requirements of the Company’s QHSE Management System. No formal qualifications required with full training provided. However preference will be given to candidates with previous experience in a clean line/workshop environment. Competencies required: Ability to understand and interpret technical instructions  Flexible approach, team player and ability to work independently What we offer you Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

jobs.title: Resident Engineer
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: Leads the delivery (time, cost, quality) of assigned package. Supervise the contractors to ensure the works are constructed as per specification and to established milestone dates. Manage and direct the supervision team to undertake day to day inspections of the works. jobs.title: Project Controls Manager - Rail
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Project Controls Manager to lead and oversee all project controls functions for a major rail infrastructure project. This role will be responsible for ensuring robust planning, scheduling, cost control, risk management, and performance reporting across the full project lifecycle. The successful candidate will bring extensive experience in the management of design and or construction phases of large scale rail projects, with a strong preference for High Speed Rail project exposure. Key Responsibilities • Lead the development and implementation of project controls frameworks, processes, and systems across the project • Oversee planning and scheduling activities, ensuring alignment with project milestones and delivery objectives • Manage cost control, budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting • Establish and monitor project KPIs, dashboards, and performance metrics • Lead risk management activities, including risk identification, analysis, mitigation, and reporting • Ensure effective change management and claims support in coordination with commercial teams • Provide strategic oversight on progress measurement and reporting to senior stakeholders • Coordinate with engineering, construction, and commercial teams to ensure integrated project delivery • Ensure compliance with client, regulatory, and governance requirements • Lead and mentor the project controls team Minimum Requirements • Minimum of fifteen (15) years of experience in project controls  • Proven experience in a Project Controls Manager or equivalent role on at least one project of similar size and complexity • Demonstrated experience of minimum 5 years in railway projects with High Speed Rail (HSR) projects being highly preferred   • Strong expertise in planning, cost management, risk management, and reporting • Proficiency in industry standard tools such as Primavera P6, cost management systems, and reporting platforms • Experience in the Middle East, particularly the UAE, is highly advantageous Key Competencies • Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills • Strategic thinking with strong analytical capability • Excellent communication and reporting skills • Ability to operate in a complex, multi stakeholder environment • High attention to detail with a proactive approach to problem solving • University degree in Engineering or a related discipline. A Master’s degree is preferred • Chartered Engineer status or equivalent professional accreditation is highly desirable

jobs.description: The Senior Audit Manager is responsible for managing audit assurance activities across key Citibank UAE businesses and products, including Wealth, Services, Markets and Banking, while also overseeing Citibank UAE cross-discipline audits. The role includes direct management of a team of Internal Audit professionals, and reports directly to the UAE Head of Audit. Participate in the annual audit planning process to ensure alignment with business risks, regulatory requirements, and emerging risks. Monitor regulatory developments to ensure audit coverage addresses applicable regulatory requirements. Deliver audit reports, audit and regulatory issue validations, business monitoring and governance committee reports. Apply professional judgement in evaluating risks and forming audit conclusions, escalate significant risks accordingly. Adhere to Internal Audit standards, policies, and regulatory requirements while providing timely and effective audit assurance; ensure established timelines and budgets are met. Contribute to continuous improvement of audit assurance processes, including development of data analytics and usage of AI tools to enhance audit efficiency and coverage. Develop and maintain effective relationships with stakeholders to ensure strong understanding of the business. Act as trusted advisor to stakeholders by providing independent challenge and risk-based insights. jobs.title: Business Execution Support Analyst (UAE Nationals Only)
jobs.description: The Business Execution Analyst will be a pivotal member of the franchise's first line of defense, responsible for driving operational excellence, strengthening the control environment, and ensuring seamless coordination of key governance and regulatory processes. This role requires a proactive and detail-oriented individual who can effectively manage multiple priorities, liaise with various stakeholders, and provide critical support to the Business Execution team and senior leadership. The successful candidate will play a key role in upholding the integrity of the franchise's risk and control framework. Key Responsibilities: I. Governance & Committee Coordination Meeting Management: Drive the end-to-end preparation and coordination for Country Coordinating Committee (CCC) meetings. This includes compiling, reviewing, and distributing executive-level materials, preparing presentation slides, and collating essential data for discussion. Action Item Tracking: Diligently track, monitor, and follow up on all tasks and action items arising from CCC meetings to ensure timely completion and resolution by the responsible parties. Annual Survey Coordination: Lead the coordination and execution of the annual CCC survey, managing the process from distribution to data analysis and the presentation of findings. Documentation: Take ownership of maintaining and updating critical documents, records, and charters owned by the franchise. II. Issue & Risk Management Lifecycle Oversight: Manage downloading issue data from the management dashboard, tracking progress, and following up with relevant teams to ensure timely activation and closure. Proactive Escalation: Provide robust oversight for all issues and Corrective Action Plans (CAPs) under the CCOBH ownership. Proactively identify and escalate potential risks, such as upcoming target dates, potential re-targets, or re-opened issues. Remediation Support: Work closely with business units to ensure all necessary approvals are secured for any issue re-targets. Collaborate with second line-of-defense functions and other partners to escalate at-risk items and secure support for remediation efforts. III. Control Environment & MCA Program Control Enhancement: Actively contribute to strengthening the overall control environment by partnering with business leaders to increase the rate of self-identified issues and foster a culture of proactive risk management. MCA Program Support: Download and analyze Country MCA reports to support the franchise in the effective monitoring and testing of key controls. Overdue Controls Management: Focus on reducing the number of overdue Regulatory Changes(RCs) to achieve a tangible reduction in compliance risk. This includes monitoring overdue RCs across the country and driving the relevant Assessment Units (AUs) toward timely resolution. ICAPs Integration: In line with Citi’s Issue Management standards, ensure that overdue RCs are appropriately escalated by following up with relevant AUs to open an official issue/CAP in the ICAPs system in a timely manner. IV. Regulatory Compliance & Engagement Deliverable Tracking: Proactively track, coordinate, and manage all regulatory deliverables to ensure deadlines are met and submissions are of high quality. Communication Logging: Meticulously maintain the regulatory communication tracker, ensuring all interactions with regulatory bodies are logged accurately and promptly. Advisory Support: Support the Independent Compliance Risk Management (ICRM) team, as applicable, in the determination and application of regulatory requirements to a specific business or function. Stakeholder Engagement: Assist the CCOBH in preparing for and managing engagements with regulatory bodies, governmental agencies, and other external stakeholders as required. General Initiatives: Provide essential support for general and strategic initiatives led by the Country and Cluster Business Execution teams. Qualifications & Desired Skills Experience: Experienc …

Understanding of Citi’s Policies, Standards, and Procedures. Good analytical skills to evaluate complex risk and control activities and processes. jobs.title: Anti Money Laundering Analyst (UAE National Only)
jobs.description: The Execution Analyst 2 is an intermediate position responsible for performing risk and compliance reviews in coordination with the broader Anti-Money Laundering (AML) team. The overall objective is to utilize in-depth AML knowledge to review, analyze and conduct effective due diligence and investigations on higher risk cases that may be a potential risk to Citi. Responsibilities: Conduct investigations and research on potentially suspicious clients using various internal and external systems and databases Conduct Cross Sector reviews among multiple Citi business lines. Document and report the review/investigation findings and prepare case files with the required supporting documentation Summarize, in writing, clear and concise findings of the investigation Advise senior management on next steps and provide recommendations on the next course of action (relationship retention, termination, Suspicious Activity Report (SAR)) Assist AML team with managing risks by analyzing the root cause of issues and impact to business Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency. jobs.description: If you’re ready to bring your knowledge from the classroom to the boardroom, Citi wants to help you get there. jobs.title: Credit Risk Analyst (UAE Nationals Only)
jobs.description: Credit RisK Analyst The Credit Risk Analyst is a developing professional role. Citi is seeking an experienced and motivated professional to join its Risk Management team as a Credit Analyst. This role plays a critical part in evaluating and improving credit risk management practices. The successful candidate will contribute to UAE Wealth Citigold/Citigold Private Client Risk team in identifying, monitoring, and mitigating emerging credit risks. This position requires strong analytical capabilities, independent judgment, and leadership experience in credit risk or related areas. The role will provide analytical and project support to Credit Risk team, help produce internal reporting, and contribute to key presentations and regulatory deliverables. The ideal candidate will have understanding of risk, experience in data analytics and management reporting. What you’ll do • Assess the effectiveness of Citi’s credit risk management practices and support identification and mitigation of credit risk across Citi’s retail portfolios • Analyse credit risks by utilizing reports, scorecards, risk systems, and expert judgment • Provide support for loss forecasting, stress testing and management reporting. • Evaluate moderately complex and variable credit issues with significant business impact • Support the preparation of Credit Portfolio Scorecards and trend analysis reports • Prepare internal presentations for management and regulatory stakeholders What we’ll need from you • 1-2 years of relevant experience in analytics, financial analysis, or a related quantitative field. • Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field • Intermediate to advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint etc.) • Strong English written and verbal communication skills • Excellent time management, organizational, and analytical abilities • Experience with SAS and other analytical programs (Python, R) is a plus By joining Citi Dubai, you will not only be part of a business casual workplace with a hybrid working model (up to 2 days working at home per week), but also receive a competitive base salary (which is annually reviewed) and enjoy a whole host of additional benefits that support you (and your family) to be well, live well and save well. Alongside these benefits Citi is committed to ensuring our workplace is where everyone feels comfortable coming to work as their whole self every day. We want the best talent around the world to be energized to join us, motivated to stay, and empowered to thrive. Sounds like Citi has everything you need? Then apply to discover the true extent of your capabilities ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family Group: Risk Management ------------------------------------------------------ Job Family: Credit Risk ------------------------------------------------------ Time Type: Full time ------------------------------------------------------ Most Relevant Skills Analytical Thinking, Constructive Debate, Escalation Management, Financial Analysis, Policy and Procedure, Policy and Regulation, Product Knowledge, Risk Controls and Monitors, Risk Identification and Assessment. ------------------------------------------------------ Other Relevant Skills For complementary skills, please see above and/or contact the recruiter. ------------------------------------------------------ Citi is an equal opportunity employer, and qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristic protected by law. Responsibilities: Regulatory Change Management: Actively collaborating with business to identify, evaluate, and manage regulatory adherence risks arising from the definition of business strategy, development of new products and services, changes in regulations or internal policies, or from business-led changes. Working with business, Legal & ICRM to facilitate regulatory change management activities by performing comprehensive impact assessments of regulatory changes on existing business processes, policies, procedures, systems, data requirements, and the overall control environment. Actively collaborate with business units to identify, evaluate, and manage regulatory adherence risks arising from new regulations or internal policy changes, documenting identified gaps and required actions. Facilitate communication and training programs on regulatory changes to ensure understanding and adherence by affected stakeholders across the organization. Track the implementation status of regulatory changes using established tools and methodologies, and report progress, challenges, and residual risks to senior management and governance committees. This includes maintaining an audit trail of decisions and actions taken. Regulatory Review & Exam Management : Serve as Controls point of contact for all regulatory examinations, audits, and inquiries from supervisory authorities, building strong working relationships. Track, monitor, and report on all regulatory findings, recommendations, and the status of remediation efforts through their lifecycle. Participate in targeted reviews and engage proactively with Internal Audit and Compliance Assurance over potential regulatory findings, contributing to a unified front. Develop and implement strategies and playbooks to address recurring examination themes and enhance overall preparedness for future regulatory engagements, including mock exams and readiness assessments. jobs.description: Are you looking for a career move that will put you at the heart of a global financial institution? Then bring your skills in expanding a high net worth client base. By Joining Citi, you will become part of a global organisation whose mission is to serve as a trusted partner to our clients by responsibly providing financial services that enable growth and economic progress. What you’ll do The overall objective is to onboard new to bank Citigold & Citigold Private clients, responsible for meeting monthly sales targets. The person will also be responsible for identifying any existing Citiblue clients who can be upgraded to Citigold, by upsizing current funding. Key Responsibilities: Jointly work with the Acquisition Head and Branch Banking Staff for identifying and acquiring Citigold new-to-bank customers, as also upgrading existing Citiblue customers to Citigold Identify and solicit new-to-bank Citigold customers, through internal and external channels Assist client in opening Citigold account in U.A.E. or in an offshore location(Singapore), as per the clients individual preference Actively promote referrals from existing clients to generate a pipeline of Citigold prospects. jobs.title: Account Executive - Portfolio - Public Sector - Abu Dhabi
jobs.description: Meet the Team : As an Account Executive for the UAE Public Sector, you will be the strategic leader driving Cisco's digital transformation initiatives across government entities and public institutions. You will own the entire sales cycle, from opportunity identification to deal closure, ensuring alignment with national digital agendas and Cisco's portfolio. Your role is pivotal in building trusted C-suite relationships and orchestrating cross-functional teams to deliver innovative, integrated solutions that address critical public service challenges. Key Responsibilities • Strategic Account Management: Develop and execute comprehensive account plans to meet and exceed sales targets, expanding Cisco's footprint within the UAE public sector. • Customer Engagement: Build and maintain strong C-level relationships, acting as a trusted advisor aligned with government digital transformation goals and regulatory requirements. • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Lead and coordinate internal teams (engineering, services, partners) to deliver end-to-end solutions tailored to public sector needs. • Contract Negotiation: Manage complex contract negotiations and oversee the full sales cycle to ensure seamless delivery and customer satisfaction. • Market Intelligence: Analyze market trends, policy changes, and procurement processes to proactively position Cisco as the preferred technology partner. • Partner Ecosystem Development: Collaborate with partners to build a robust ecosystem that supports government procurement and project execution. • Sales Tools Utilization: Leverage Cisco's technology stack and AI-driven sales tools to enhance sales efficiency and pipeline accuracy. Minimum Qualifications • Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, or a related Technical field. • Minimum 10 years of technology sales experience, with a focus on Public Sector or Government accounts in the UAE or Middle East. • Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales quotas in the region. • Fluency in English (written and verbal) and proficiency in Arabic. • Deep understanding of UAE public sector procurement, regulatory environments, and government digital transformation initiatives. Preferred Qualifications • Master's degree or MBA. • Experience selling cloud, security, and software-defined networking solutions. • Established network of relationships within UAE government and semi-government organizations. • Strong presentation and storytelling skills, capable of simplifying complex technical concepts for diverse stakeholders. • Ability to work effectively in a matrixed organization and lead through influence. Why Cisco? At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint. Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere. We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

jobs.title: Senior Draughtsman (BIM Coordinator)
jobs.description: Prepare and revise technical drawings, layouts, and as-built documentation using the latest versions of MicroStation and AutoCAD. Interpret design inputs from engineers and accurately translate them into detailed and coordinated construction drawings. Review, update, and maintain drawings in compliance with project specifications, authority requirements (RTA, DM, DEWA), and AECOM quality standards. Track and verify existing sewerage, stormwater, and irrigation networks in Dubai using as-built records from Drainage (DS), Irrigation (IRR), and Roads projects. Conduct site surveys and coordinate with inspectors to verify construction progress and collect accurate data for as-built documentation. Coordinate closely with engineers, inspectors, and document control teams to ensure timely submission and approval of drawings. Update and prepare redline markups, section details, pipeline profiles, and microtunneling shaft layouts based on field data. Maintain a well-organized archive and digital record system for all project drawings and revisions. Review contractors’ as-built and shop drawing submissions to verify compliance with actual site conditions. Provide technical drafting support during construction to ensure drawings reflect approved changes and actual site execution. Support the preparation of utility coordination drawings, ensuring accuracy across multiple services and avoiding conflicts. Ensure all drawing deliverables meet AECOM and authority CAD standards, including layering, scaling, and title block consistency. Bachelor's Degree in Geography, Civil & Survey Engineering, Geomatics Engineering or related curriculum (or equivalent) and typically 6+ years of related experience Minimum of 10 years of experience in BIM and GIS engineering roles. Proficiency in BIM Software: Mastery of BIM software platforms such as Autodesk Civil 3D, Autodesk MAP,  Bentley MicroStation, Infraworks,  is essential for creating and managing building models Strong command of GIS software such as Esri ArcGIS, QGIS,  Autodesk Autocad, Revit / Navisworks experience

jobs.title: Quantity Surveyor
jobs.description: We're looking for an experienced and detail-oriented Quantity Surveyor to join our dynamic team in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates. As a Quantity Surveyor, you will combine analytical expertise with collaborative problem-solving to deliver exceptional value to our clients and stakeholders. Your meticulous attention to detail, combined with your innovative approach to cost management, will be instrumental in driving project success in the competitive UAE construction market. jobs.title: Planning Engineer
jobs.description: ·       Develop, review, and analyze project schedules, ensuring proper scope understanding, quantities, and logical sequencing of construction activities. Review and monitor the Contractor’s baseline programme , procurement schedules, and construction plans, including subcontractors and utilities. Work closely with the Contractor’s planners to track progress and recommend updates or recovery plans where required. Monitor actual progress against the approved programme and report deviations to the Senior Resident Engineer . Prepare weekly and monthly reports , including progress updates, graphs, and critical path analysis for the PMC. Identify project risks, delays, and opportunities , and recommend mitigation strategies to maintain the project schedule. Conduct site visits and coordinate with supervision teams to validate progress and ensure accurate reporting. Attend workshops and meetings to assess real-time progress and resolve planning/scheduling issues. Support the Senior Quantity Surveyor in analyzing programme-related impacts of variations and additional works. Maintain detailed planning records to support evaluation of claims, variations, and dispute resolution processes. Assist the PMC in negotiations, arbitration, and other dispute resolution matters related to project delays or planning issues. Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering or related field . Minimum 10 years of planning and scheduling experience , with strong focus on Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) projects . Proven experience in construction planning, scheduling, and progress monitoring for complex infrastructure/STP works. UAE project experience preferred , with understanding of local authority processes and project requirements. Proficiency in planning tools such as Primavera P6 or equivalent .

jobs.title: Assistant Resident Engineer
jobs.description: • Review Inspectors’ Daily Reports to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with project standards. • Prepare the Engineer’s Daily Diary summarizing daily activities, key progress, and observations. • Provide general oversight of Inspectors’ daily activities, including task assignments, on-site performance, and technical guidance. • Review Contractor’s inspection, surveying, and testing requests and evaluate subsequent reports for conformance with specifications. • Review and approve Contractor’s shop drawings, materials, and methods as delegated by the Resident Engineer. • Monitor and verify the performance of the Contractor’s survey team and ensure accuracy of setting-out works. • Prepare meeting minutes and technical correspondence as delegated by the Resident Engineer. • Coordinate with the Contractor’s supervisory staff regarding work sequence, methodology, and schedule compliance. • Monitor construction progress and ensure adherence to the Contractor’s approved baseline program. • Conduct continuous field inspections of the work in progress and provide consultation to both the Resident Engineer and Contractor’s teams. • Prepare written and verbal reports for the Resident Engineer on technical, contractual, and procedural matters. • Review and verify as-built drawings in coordination with inspectors and surveyors. • Ensure all site activities are executed in accordance with approved drawings, project specifications, and HSE standards. • Identify non-conformances, initiate corrective actions, and follow up to ensure resolution. • Support the Resident Engineer in reviewing technical submittals, progress reports, and project documentation. • Maintain effective coordination between the supervision team and the Contractor to resolve site-related issues efficiently. Liaise and coordinate with relevant Dubai authorities such as RTA, DM, DEWA, Etisalat, DU, and Civil Defense for inspections, and NOCs. • Review authority-related drawings and ensure compliance with each authority’s latest standards and specifications. • Follow up on authority inspection requests and assist in resolving comments or non-compliance issues. • Ensure timely submission and approval of all authority NOCs required for construction, testing, and final handover. • Coordinate with utility service providers to facilitate connection works, diversions, and protection of existing services. • Attend authority meetings and provide technical input to support the Resident Engineer during coordination and progress discussions. • Maintain records of authority communications, approvals, and correspondence for project documentation and handover. Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline from a recognized university. Minimum 10 years of extensive experience in Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) projects , covering construction supervision, installation, testing, and commissioning of treatment facilities and associated infrastructure. Proven, hands-on involvement in end-to-end delivery of STP works , including civil, mechanical, and process-related components, with a strong understanding of treatment systems and operational requirements. UAE project experience is highly preferred , with in-depth knowledge of local authority regulations, approval processes, and standards related specifically to STP and infrastructure projects. Strong expertise in construction supervision practices , including review of shop drawings, material submittals, inspection/testing procedures, and as-built documentation within STP environments. Solid understanding of UAE codes, environmental regulations, and HSE requirements applicable to wastewater treatment facilities. Demonstrated ability to coordinate effectively with contractors, consultants, and authorities to ensure successful and timely delivery of STP projects .

jobs.title: Senior Advisor - Safety, Health & Environment
jobs.description: We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Senior Advisor - Safety, Health & Environment to join our dynamic team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. In this pivotal role, you will champion a culture of safety and environmental responsibility across our organization, providing strategic guidance and technical expertise to ensure compliance with international standards and local regulations. As a visionary leader, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop, implement, and continuously improve comprehensive safety and environmental programs that protect our workforce and stakeholders. Develop, implement, and maintain integrated Safety, Health & Environment (SHE) programs and policies aligned with organizational objectives and regulatory requirements Conduct comprehensive hazard identification, risk assessments, and analysis to evaluate unsafe conditions and practices across all operational areas Design and oversee hazard control processes, methods, and preventive programs that mitigate workplace risks and promote a proactive safety culture Lead incident investigations to identify root causes of accidents, near-misses, and safety violations; prepare detailed reports with corrective and preventive action recommendations Perform regular safety audits and inspections of work sites, facilities, and processes; document findings and communicate corrective actions to relevant stakeholders Develop and deliver engaging safety training programs, inductions, and awareness sessions for employees, contractors, and visitors across all organizational levels Monitor and track safety performance metrics, key performance indicators (KPIs), and safety statistics; prepare monthly and periodic reports for management review Ensure organizational compliance with ISO 14000/14001 standards, local UAE regulations, and applicable federal and state requirements Collaborate with engineering, quality assurance, regulatory compliance, and operational teams to integrate SHE considerations into project planning and execution Review and advise on safety requirements in method statements, activity hazard analyses, and permit-to-work procedures Maintain and verify proper maintenance, calibration, and operation of all safety equipment and systems Lead regular SHE meetings with internal teams and external contractors; facilitate open communication regarding safety concerns and best practices Develop and implement environmental management strategies including waste disposal, pollution control, recycling initiatives, and resource conservation Represent management during regulatory inspections and audits; maintain transparent communication with authorities and ensure adherence to compliance standards Stay current with evolving safety regulations, industry best practices, and technological innovations; recommend process improvements and innovative solutions Foster an inclusive and supportive safety culture that empowers employees to identify hazards and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives Mentor and support junior safety personnel and team members in developing their technical competencies and professional growth Prepare comprehensive SHE plans based on historical data, lessons learned, and forward-looking risk analysis Perform additional responsibilities as assigned to support organizational goals and operational excellence Minimum 10 years of progressive experience in Safety, Health & Environment roles within corporate or industrial organizations, with strong exposure to Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) projects . Engineering Diploma or equivalent qualification as a minimum educational requirement UAE experience is highly preferred , with sound knowledge of local regulations and standards.. Relevant HSE certifications such as NEBOSH (National Examination Board in Occupational Safety and Health) or IOSH (Institution of Occupational Safety and Health) Demonstrated expertise in hazard identification, risk assessment, and development of ha …
