jobs.title: Senior Engineer - Structural
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: We are looking for a Senior Engineer - Structural role. Responsible for overseeing and managing the implementation of construction documents on-site, while supporting discipline engineers across other projects as needed. Provides technical support to the ARE/RE and coordinates with the design office, contractor, and client representatives to ensure construction aligns with approved plans and specifications. The incumbent performs a broad cross-section of complex technical and administrative activities requiring extensive experience and specialization in Contract Compliance, Quality Control (QC) and Quality Assurance (QA) inspection of structural scope related to the project Analyses applicable codes, standards and project contract documents and monitors construction activities to ensure compliance with contract documents. Interprets or clarifies contract documents for Contractors. Maintains and prepares a variety of records, including daily/weekly progress reports, material accounting records, change orders and record drawings. Provide assistance to the Project Engineer/Site Engineer. Responsible for supervising, directing and coordinating the inspection team. Conduct weekly meetings with inspectors and Project Engineer. Ensuring that responses are complete and in compliance with the contract documents. Where problems are found, work with Project Engineer to resolve. Audit inspections for conformity to contract documents and contractor submitted plans. Assist the Senior Discipline engineer to identify, respond to or evaluate technical issues to maintain the schedule. Highlights any violations to construction/ engineering related issues on site through issuance of NCRs Attends progress review meetings and coordination meetings to assist the Project Lead/Senior Engineer  in resolving any site issues, such as discrepancies in project’s drawings.                                                                Participates in the coordination and monitoring of snagging and project handover Provide support to the Resident Engineer/Team lead to ensure projects run smoothly by providing expert knowledge and advice. Liaises with the Design Engineer on modifications if required. Gives daily site reports and highlights issues that arise on site to the Project Lead. Contributes in the preparation of all kinds of reports and as directed by the project lead. Monitor Health and Safety of the construction works and report on the same. If required stop the construction works if unsafe practices are being followed. Bachelor Degree in relevant Engineering field (Civil or Structural) Minimum 15 years of relevant experience in building construction projects.

jobs.title: Senior BIM Engineer - Site
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: The Senior BIM Engineer – Supervision is responsible for overseeing the implementation and management of Building Information Modelling (BIM) processes on the projects. This role ensures the delivery of high-quality digital outputs, alignment with project requirements. The position requires strong coordination, technical expertise, and stakeholder engagement to drive digital excellence throughout the project lifecycle.  Job Duties: Develop, implement, and enforce BIM standards, workflows, and execution plans in the projects. Ensure all BIM models and shop drawings comply with company, client, and project-specific requirements. Lead the integration of BIM processes into project delivery, ensuring alignment with digital engineering strategies. Act as the primary point of contact for BIM-related coordination among architects, engineers, contractors, and other stakeholders. Facilitate cross-disciplinary collaboration to resolve BIM-related issues and ensure seamless model integration. Support project teams in effectively interpreting and applying BIM standards. Oversee the creation, maintenance, and updating of BIM models and associated digital deliverables. Conduct regular quality control checks to ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with specifications. Implement procedures for model audits, clash detection, and data validation to ensure accuracy and integrity. Provide guidance, training, and technical support to project teams on BIM tools, workflows, and best practices. Manage BIM software tools, including installation, configuration, licensing, and troubleshooting. Evaluate emerging technologies and recommend the adoption of innovative digital solutions to enhance project delivery. Stay current with industry trends, standards, and advancements in digital engineering. Develop and manage BIM execution plans tailored to each project's specific needs. Monitor progress against BIM deliverables to ensure timely and budget-compliant outcomes. Support project reporting and documentation related to digital engineering activities. Strong understanding of construction processes, digital workflows, and data management. Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field. Extensive experience with BIM software such as Revit, AutoCAD, and Navisworks. Strong understanding of construction processes and shop drawing requirements. Strong experience in ISO 19650 Strong working experience in BIM methodologies Strong experience in AutoCAD Civil 3D Excellent communication and coordination skills. Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines. Minimum of 15 years of experience.

jobs.title: Resident Engineer - Freight Facilities
jobs.description: AECOM is seeking an experienced Resident Engineer for a major Freight Facilities Project located in Northern Emirates. The Resident Engineer will be responsible for leading and managing the site supervision team and ensuring that all construction activities are executed in accordance with the contract requirements, project specifications, approved drawings, quality standards, safety requirements, and project schedule. The successful candidate will oversee the delivery of infrastructure and freight facility works, coordinate with stakeholders and authorities, manage contractor performance, and ensure compliance with project objectives and international best practices. Job Duties Lead and manage the site supervision team for the Freight Facilities Project in Northern Emirates. Monitor the Contractor’s work progress against the approved construction programme and identify potential delays or risks. Ensure compliance with Contract Documents, specifications, approved drawings, and project procedures. Supervise construction activities related to freight facilities, roads, utilities, logistics yards, service corridors, drainage, and associated infrastructure works. Review and approve method statements, shop drawings, material submittals, RFIs, and technical submissions. Coordinate with Clients, Authorities, Contractors, Stakeholders, and other consultants to ensure smooth project execution. Monitor site safety implementation and ensure compliance with project SH&E requirements and safety regulations. Conduct regular site inspections and audits to verify quality of workmanship and materials. Review and certify contractor interim payment applications. Evaluate and prepare Variation Orders, Change Orders, Claims, and Extension of Time recommendations in accordance with the Contract. Monitor testing and commissioning activities and ensure compliance with project specifications. Maintain proper documentation and records related to progress, quality, safety, inspections, and contractual matters. Prepare and review weekly and monthly progress reports. Support project close-out activities, including snagging, testing & commissioning, as-built drawings, and handover documentation. Ensure effective coordination between all engineering disciplines and stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. Mentor and guide junior engineers and inspectors within the supervision team.       Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from an accredited university. Chartered Engineer status or equivalent professional accreditation is preferred.   Specific Skills Required Strong experience in construction supervision of freight facilities, logistics hubs, industrial infrastructure, roads, utilities, and heavy civil works. Excellent understanding of FIDIC Contracts, particularly Red Book conditions. Strong knowledge of project planning, construction methodologies, contract administration, and claim management. Experience in coordinating with local authorities and stakeholders in UAE. Ability to manage multidisciplinary supervision teams on large-scale infrastructure projects. Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Proficient in modern construction management software and reporting systems. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.   Experience Minimum 20 years of overall experience in construction supervision and infrastructure projects. Minimum 10 years of experience as Resident Engineer on large-scale infrastructure or industrial developments. Previous experience in freight facilities, logistics infrastructure, industrial zones, ports, or major transportation projects is highly preferred. GCC/UAE experience is mandatory, preferably within Northern Emirates region projects.

jobs.title: Inspector - Architectural
jobs.location: Ras al Khaimah, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: Monitoring and ensuring that all architectural engineering aspects of the project are carried out as per inspection plan/drawings/codes and specifications. Liaising with the Senior Architectural  Engineer and other Engineers and Managers to provide support and guidance as required Review and monitor variations to the work to mitigate time and cost impacts for any required changes Monitor the review of the contractor’s documents/shop drawings to flag and rectify any issues which may affect the stability or function of any architectural components or systems. Monitor contractor’s compliance with approved Quality Control Plan. Monitor the project related protocol set up for Inspection of works by the inspection teams. Monitor safety aspects when dealing with architectural  installations Provide reports on the progress of the architectural aspects of the project. Assist in expediting of NOCs from relevant authorities if they appear to be stalled or in delay Maintain full site records, including daily site diaries in an approved format, and formal monthly progress photography. Expedition of approvals from the Client if it appears to be stalled or delaying the project Liaising with the management of the various contractors to expedite submissions and approvals process. Perform and or evaluate tests confirming quality requirements, architectural finishes and building components by conducting thorough site inspection for all architectural works as per approved shop drawings, specifications and method statement. Field checks placement, location and quality of architectural finishes, building structure components. Monitor contractor’s compliance with approved Quality Control Plan. Coordinating with the team on site to ensure that contractual obligations are met. Perform Architectural inspections and material inspections. Issue NCRs to contractors and assist in taking corrective actions. Ensure that all inspection reports are accurate and in compliance with the requirements. Ensure that all execution and inspection are carried out as per inspection plan/drawings/codes and specifications. Ensure compliance of architectural works to design requirements, specifications and project plan. Preparing daily logs and reports. Maintain records of all submittals and testing inspections made, tests performed, work executed, etc. Performing all work in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Assisting with the inspection of completed works. Perform all other duties given by direct manager. Monitoring and reporting on HSE related matters and participating in HSE training and meetings   Bachelor Degree or Diploma in Architecture or relevant degree Minimum 5 to 7 years of experience in building construction

jobs.title: Document Controller
jobs.description: Maintain and control all project documents, drawings, and correspondence in accordance with company procedures. Ensure proper document numbering, filing, tracking, and distribution systems are maintained. Receive, register, and distribute incoming and outgoing documents. Maintain updated logs for: Drawings RFIs Submittals NCRs Material approvals Technical submissions Correspondence Ensure only latest approved revisions of drawings and documents are available at site. Coordinate with engineers, consultants, contractors, and project teams for document submissions and approvals. Upload and manage documents through client systems and EDMS platforms. Track pending submissions and follow up for approvals and responses. Maintain hard copy and electronic filing systems for easy retrieval of records. Prepare transmittals for document submissions and distribution. Archive superseded and obsolete documents according to project procedures. Ensure confidentiality and security of project records and company documents. Assist project team in preparation of reports, presentations, and project documentation. Support QA/QC team in maintaining quality records and inspection documents. Maintain accurate records of shop drawings, method statements, and material submittals. Coordinate document flow between site office, head office, consultant, and client. Prepare weekly and monthly document status reports. Ensure compliance with project document control procedures and ISO standards. Monitor deadlines for submissions and maintain document turnaround tracking. Assist during audits by providing required documentation and records. Maintain attendance records, staff documents, and administrative files if required. Ensure all handover documents and close-out records are properly compiled and archived. jobs.title: Engineer - Civil (Rail, Track)
jobs.description: Supervise and monitor railway track construction activities in compliance with approved drawings, specifications, and project requirements. Coordinate track works including: Ballasted track Ballastless/slab track Rail installation Sleeper installation Turnouts and crossings Track alignment and geometry Review and verify shop drawings, method statements, and material submittals related to track works. Ensure track construction complies with railway standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. Monitor earthworks, formation preparation, and subgrade conditions before track installation. Coordinate with survey teams for track alignment, levels, and setting out verification. Supervise rail welding, fastening systems, and track fixing activities. Coordinate interface works with: Civil structures Stations Utilities Signaling systems MEP systems Ensure compliance with quality assurance and quality control procedures. Review inspection requests and ensure timely closure of WIRs and inspection records. Raise NCRs and site observations for non-compliant works. Coordinate with client, consultant, contractor, and railway authorities during execution. Ensure implementation of approved safety procedures and railway operational safety requirements. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track systems. Participate in track inspections, snagging, and handover activities. Assist Resident Engineer and Project Manager in resolving technical and construction issues. Ensure compliance with international railway standards and project specifications. Witness field testing activities including compaction tests, concrete tests, rail ultrasonic testing, and track geometry measurements. Ensure all materials used on site are approved and comply with project specifications and international railway standards. Coordinate with surveyors to verify track coordinates, alignment, and elevations. Monitor contractor’s daily site activities and ensure work progress follows approved construction schedules. Raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs), site observations, and deficiency reports when required. Verify corrective actions and ensure closure of NCRs and inspection comments. Ensure all inspections and testing activities are properly documented and recorded. Coordinate with engineers, contractors, subcontractors, and client representatives for inspection activities. Monitor implementation of health, safety, and environmental requirements during railway construction activities. Inspect drainage works, cable troughs, utility crossings, and associated rail infrastructure works. Ensure protection of existing railway assets and utilities during construction activities. Attend site meetings, progress meetings, and technical coordination meetings as required. Prepare daily inspection reports and maintain proper QA/QC documentation records. Support testing and commissioning activities related to track and rail systems. Ensure compliance with relevant railway authority requirements, standards, and regulations. Diploma in Civil Engineering or an equivalent qualification from a recognized institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with a strong focus on rail track works, track installation, and associated structural components Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects , particularly in rail, metro, or heavy transportation systems Demonstrated knowledge of railway construction specifications, approved drawings, and Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs) related to track works Strong understanding of track alignment, ballast placement and compaction, rail fastening systems, and concrete works associated with track infrastructure Familiarity with rail material quality control procedures , including inspection of rails, sleepers, ballast, sub-ballast, and related components Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports, material approvals, and non-conformance documentation (NCRs) Excellent command of written and spoken English for effective site communication and reporting

Ensure proper document numbering, filing, tracking, and distribution systems are maintained. Receive, register, and distribute incoming and outgoing documents. Coordinate with engineers, consultants, contractors, and project teams for document submissions and approvals. Upload and manage documents through client systems and EDMS platforms. Track pending submissions and follow up for approvals and responses. Maintain hard copy and electronic filing systems for easy retrieval of records. Prepare transmittals for document submissions and distribution. Archive superseded and obsolete documents according to project procedures. Ensure confidentiality and security of project records and company documents. Assist project team in preparation of reports, presentations, and project documentation. Support QA/QC team in maintaining quality records and inspection documents. Maintain accurate records of shop drawings, method statements, and material submittals. Coordinate document flow between site office, head office, consultant, and client. Prepare weekly and monthly document status reports. Ensure compliance with project document control procedures and ISO standards. Monitor deadlines for submissions and maintain document turnaround tracking. Ensure all handover documents and close-out records are properly compiled and archived. Diploma or Bachelor’s in Business/Engineering or related field. 7+ years’ document control experience on major infrastructure projects (rail/freight/industrial preferred). Proficient with EDMS platforms (e.g., Aconex, Asite, ProjectWise, ACC/BIM 360) and MS Office; strong Excel skills. jobs.title: Engineer - Civil (Rail, Track)
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Civil Engineer specializing in freight infrastructure to join our team in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.  You will work collaboratively with project teams to ensure all projects meet regulatory standards, safety requirements, and operational efficiency goals while maintaining organized project documentation and timely delivery. Supervise and monitor construction in line with approved IFC drawings, specifications, method statements, and project requirements. Review and verify shop drawings, method statements, ITPs, and material submittals for track works and related systems. Monitor earthworks, formation preparation, drainage, and subgrade condition ahead of track installation. Coordinate with survey teams on alignment, levels, control points, and setting‑out verification. Coordinate interfaces with civil structures, stations, utilities, signaling, and MEP to protect program and quality. Enforce QA/QC procedures; review WIRs, manage inspections, and ensure timely close‑out and records. Raise and manage NCRs and site observations; track corrective and preventive actions to closure. Monitor contractor progress versus the approved baseline schedule; highlight risks, slippage, and recovery plans. Prepare accurate daily, weekly, and monthly progress reports with metrics, photos, and look‑ahead. Coordinate with the client, consultant, contractor, and railway authorities during execution and inspections. Ensure implementation of approved HSE procedures and railway operational safety requirements. Support testing and commissioning for track systems; participate in inspections, snagging, and handover. Assist the Resident Engineer and Project Manager in resolving technical and construction issues. Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or related discipline; postgraduate or rail-specific qualifications are an advantage. 10+ years of construction and supervision experience on major rail or freight projects Proficient in coordinating multidisciplinary interfaces (civil, stations, utilities, signaling, MEP) and dealing with rail authorities. Skilled in progress tracking, risk identification, and recovery planning against an approved baseline schedule. GCC/UAE experience is a must.

jobs.title: Inspector - Civil ( Earthworks / Structures )

Raise NCRs (Non-Conformance Reports) and site observations for non-compliant works. Monitor contractor productivity and progress against approved baseline program. Ensure all works are carried out according to approved safety and quality procedures. Maintain daily inspection reports and site records. Coordinate with contractor, consultant, surveyor, and utility teams during execution. Inspect temporary traffic diversions and traffic management arrangements. Verify reinstatement works after utility crossings and road cutting activities. Ensure proper protection of existing utilities and road assets during construction. Attend site meetings and provide technical feedback on ongoing activities. Check as-built drawings and ensure accuracy before submission. Ensure compliance with local authority requirements such as Roads and Transport Authority and Dubai Municipality standards. Monitor drainage works associated with road construction including catch basins, manholes, and pipelines. Ensure housekeeping and environmental compliance at site. Support Resident Engineer and Project Engineer in overall site supervision and quality control activities. *Required Qualifications:** Diploma in Civil Engineering or equivalent qualification from a recognised institution Minimum 7 years of professional experience in civil construction inspection, with focus on earthworks and structural works Proven experience in large-scale infrastructure projects, including rail, transportation, or similar heavy civil works Demonstrated knowledge of construction specifications, approved drawings, and inspection test plans (ITP) Strong understanding of soil mechanics, compaction testing, and concrete quality control procedures Proficiency in preparing and maintaining inspection reports and non-conformance documentation Excellent command of written and spoken English for site communication and report preparation

jobs.title: Inspector - Materials
jobs.description: We are hiring a materials Inspector in Ruwais for a freight project, You will be responsible for inspections, materials verification, documentation, and quality control. Duties Inspect site conditions prior to the start of construction activities to verify the compatibility of the site with the approved design and permanent works. Check for obstructions and document existing site conditions through photographs to prevent potential damage to existing utilities, structures, and landscaping. Review and verify materials delivered to the site for conformance with approved samples, specifications, and equipment lists; report any non-conformances to the Resident Engineer (RE). Ensure that all materials are properly stored and protected at the site to maintain quality and prevent damage or contamination. Inspect construction of both temporary and permanent works to confirm compliance with contract documents, approved shop drawings, and project specifications. Prepare and maintain accurate daily inspection logs, test reports, and site records in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. Monitor site safety practices and ensure that all works are carried out following approved safety procedures; report unsafe practices or incidents to the RE immediately. Issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) and Site Observation Reports (SORs) for any deviation from approved materials, specifications, or procedures, and follow up on corrective actions. Assist in the inspection of completed works, prepare punch lists, and monitor testing, commissioning, and handover activities to ensure quality compliance. Review and provide recommendations on Contractors’ Requests for Information (RFIs) related to civil materials and testing requirements. Evaluate requests for material variances or substitutions and provide technical comments and recommendations to the RE for approval. Read and interpret working drawings and related specifications, enforce compliance on-site, and prepare inspection and material testing reports. Coordinate with third-party testing laboratories and ensure all material tests are performed as per project specifications and standards. Ensure that all material approvals, test results, NCRs, and inspection records are properly documented and maintained for monthly reporting and project close-out. Coordinate with relevant authorities for material approvals, testing, and compliance verification. Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or related field. Minimum of 7+ years of professional experience  working as a Material Inspector, with a strong focus on large-scale infrastructure projects. Candidates must have proven experience in: Rail and Metro projects  (such as UAE Metro, Etihad Rail, or similar international rail systems) Freight yard, rail or track infrastructure projects , including track works, ballast installation, and rail system components Strong knowledge of international and regional standards for civil materials and testing (e.g., ASTM, AASHTO, BS/EN) and QA/QC processes (ITPs, WIRs, NCRs, SORs). Proven experience reviewing shop drawings, method statements, material submittals, and managing third‑party testing labs. Familiarity with authority processes in the UAE/GCC Excellent documentation, reporting, and stakeholder coordination skills; proficiency with common CDE/reporting tools is an advantage.

You will work collaboratively with engineering and operations teams to identify risks, implement solutions, and maintain the highest standards of system integrity. Ensure civil works for rail infrastructure comply with systems assurance principles, including safety, reliability, availability, and maintainability (RAMS) throughout the project lifecycle. Implement and manage assurance processes in line with railway standards. Develop and maintain the Systems Assurance Plan covering design, construction, testing, and commissioning phases. Ensure full requirements traceability from employer requirements through design, construction, and final handover. Review civil design submissions to ensure compliance with safety-in-design and operational safety requirements for rail systems. Coordinate closely with track, signaling, MEP, utilities, and systems teams to manage civil interface requirements. Conduct and support hazard identification (HAZID), risk assessments, and safety analysis for civil railway assets (stations, viaducts, tunnels, trackworks). Ensure implementation of Verification & Validation (V&V) processes for all civil deliverables and construction activities. Monitor compliance with railway authority standards. Review Method Statements, Inspection & Test Plans (ITPs), and assurance deliverables for compliance with system safety requirements. Participate in design and construction safety audits, assurance audits, and site surveillance activities. Track and close out safety-related non-conformances, audit findings, and assurance observations. Ensure proper management of design changes through configuration control and impact assessment on safety and performance. Support integration of civil works with railway systems during testing, integration, and commissioning stages. Verify that construction activities do not compromise operational safety, track geometry stability, or long-term durability. Ensure implementation of risk mitigation measures identified during safety and assurance studies. Support incident investigation and root cause analysis for civil-related safety or quality issues. Maintain assurance registers including risk logs, compliance matrices, and traceability records. Assist in preparation of safety cases, assurance evidence, and certification dossiers for handover to the client. Coordinate with contractors, consultants, and client representatives to ensure assurance requirements are clearly understood and implemented on site. Contribute to final system acceptance, operational readiness reviews, and handover of civil railway assets. You will demonstrate exceptional organizational skills, technical expertise, and a commitment to maintaining the highest safety and quality standards across utility operations. While the role focuses on civil works for wet utilities (water, drainage, sewer), you will coordinate with dry utilities stakeholders where applicable. Key Responsibilities • Conduct routine, scheduled, and complaint-based inspections of utility infrastructure, with primary emphasis on water distribution, stormwater/drainage, and sewerage networks; coordinate with electrical, gas, and telecommunications stakeholders as applicable to interface points. • Document inspection findings in detail and prepare comprehensive, accurate inspection reports using established protocols and software systems. • Identify code violations, safety hazards, maintenance deficiencies, and areas requiring immediate attention or remediation. • Verify compliance with local UAE regulations, industry standards, and established safety protocols during all inspections. • Coordinate with maintenance, repair, and operations teams to communicate findings and recommend corrective actions. • Maintain organized and up-to-date inspection records, databases, and documentation for audit and reference purposes. • Respond promptly to stakeholder complaints and service requests by conducting follow-up inspections and investigations. • Provide technical recommendations for system repairs, upgrades, or replacements based on inspection findings. • Collaborate with regulatory agencies and stakeholders to ensure adherence to utility service standards. • Participate in training programs to stay current with evolving regulations and inspection best practices. • Plan and execute inspections in operational environments with restricted access, aligning activities with site safety, permit-to-work requirements, and operational windows typically associated with logistics or freight-adjacent corridors. • Handle sensitive information discreetly and maintain confidentiality regarding project scope, operations, and locations. Minimum 5 years of professional experience in utilities inspection, infrastructure assessment, or related field Strong technical knowledge of utility systems including water, electrical, gas, and telecommunications infrastructure Experience working on a freight facility

jobs.title: Land Surveyor
jobs.description: We are recruiting a detail‑oriented, analytical Land Surveyor to support freight facility projects across Ruwais and the wider UAE. You will ensure accuracy and precision across all surveying operations for freight facilities, collaborating with engineers, contractors, and operators to deliver safe, high‑quality outcomes in fast‑paced, live‑operations environments. If you thrive on precision and want to shape resilient, efficient freight infrastructure. Key Responsibilities Perform comprehensive survey work under the direction of senior surveying professionals, establishing primary horizontal and vertical control points with precision and accuracy Record all measurements, rod readings, and calculations meticulously in field books as work progresses, ensuring complete documentation and transparency Conduct pre-construction surveys to verify site conditions, identify potential obstructions, and support design verification processes Provide expert input and support for construction layout, staking, and setting out for roads, pavements, curbs, drainage systems, and utilities Monitor and verify contractor work alignment, levels, and grading against design drawings, maintaining rigorous quality standards Coordinate effectively with the contractor's surveyor and Resident Engineer on work priorities, measurements, and any discrepancies that arise Maintain survey equipment in optimal working condition and perform frequent calibration checks to ensure reliability and accuracy Utilize modern computer software, GPS technology, total stations, and advanced survey equipment for precise measurements and data collection Assist in preparing as-built drawings and maintain comprehensive documentation of survey records, reports, and maps for project reporting Check all work by completing traverse closures and bench circuits, demonstrating commitment to accuracy and professional excellence Ensure strict compliance with all safety procedures and protocols during survey operations, prioritizing the well-being of the team Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to resolve challenges and contribute innovative solutions to surveying obstacles What you will bring Proven land surveying experience on freight facilities. Proficiency with total stations, GNSS/GPS, digital/automatic levels, and common survey software; strong control and QA/verification skills. Familiarity with tolerances, drainage falls, slab flatness, and interface requirements typical of heavy‑use freight yards. Strong coordination and communication skills in fast‑paced, live‑operations settings. Commitment to safety, quality, and complete survey documentation. Bachelor's Degree or Diploma in Civil Engineering, Geodetic Engineering, or a related engineering field 7-10 years of professional experience as a Land Surveyor, with demonstrated expertise in construction and infrastructure projects Experience working on freight facilities

jobs.title: Resident Engineer – Freight Facilities
jobs.description: AECOM is seeking an experienced Resident Engineer for a major Freight Facilities Project located in Ruwais. The Resident Engineer will be responsible for leading and managing the site supervision team and ensuring that all construction activities are executed in accordance with the contract requirements, project specifications, approved drawings, quality standards, safety requirements, and project schedule. The successful candidate will oversee the delivery of infrastructure and freight facility works, coordinate with stakeholders and authorities, manage contractor performance, and ensure compliance with project objectives and international best practices. Job Duties Lead and manage the site supervision team for the Freight Facilities Project  Monitor the Contractor’s work progress against the approved construction programme and identify potential delays or risks. Ensure compliance with Contract Documents, specifications, approved drawings, and project procedures. Supervise construction activities related to freight facilities, roads, utilities, logistics yards, service corridors, drainage, and associated infrastructure works. Review and approve method statements, shop drawings, material submittals, RFIs, and technical submissions. Coordinate with Clients, Authorities, Contractors, Stakeholders, and other consultants to ensure smooth project execution. Monitor site safety implementation and ensure compliance with project SH&E requirements and safety regulations. Conduct regular site inspections and audits to verify quality of workmanship and materials. Review and certify contractor interim payment applications. Evaluate and prepare Variation Orders, Change Orders, Claims, and Extension of Time recommendations in accordance with the Contract. Monitor testing and commissioning activities and ensure compliance with project specifications. Maintain proper documentation and records related to progress, quality, safety, inspections, and contractual matters. Prepare and review weekly and monthly progress reports. Support project close-out activities, including snagging, testing & commissioning, as-built drawings, and handover documentation. Ensure effective coordination between all engineering disciplines and stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. Mentor and guide junior engineers and inspectors within the supervision team. ·        Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from an accredited university. Chartered Engineer status or equivalent professional accreditation is preferred. PMP certification or equivalent is an advantage. ·                Specific Skills Required Strong experience in construction supervision of freight facilities, logistics hubs, industrial infrastructure, roads, utilities, and heavy civil works. Excellent understanding of FIDIC Contracts, particularly Red Book conditions. Strong knowledge of project planning, construction methodologies, contract administration, and claim management. Experience in coordinating with local authorities and stakeholders in UAE Ability to manage multidisciplinary supervision teams on large-scale infrastructure projects. Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Proficient in modern construction management software and reporting systems. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English. ·                Experience Minimum 20 years of overall experience in construction supervision and infrastructure projects. Minimum 10 years of experience as Resident Engineer on large-scale infrastructure or industrial developments. Previous experience in freight facilities, logistics infrastructure, industrial zones, ports, or major transportation projects is highly preferred. Experience of working in a remo …

jobs.title: Senior Resident Engineer
jobs.description: AECOM is seeking an experienced Resident Engineer for a major Roads and Bridges project located in the UAE. The Resident Engineer will be responsible for the activities and efficiency of his staff for the satisfactory prosecution of the work under his supervision. He has the responsibility to build and maintain an organization that can effectively provide the necessary staking, testing and inspection to ensure the project is constructed according to the plans and specifications. He/she will familiarize rapidly with the plans, specifications and amendments to effectively administer the work. Job Duties Monitor the client’s programme or progress report Ensure the Client’s compliance with the Contract Documents. Inspect all workmanship and material. Coordinate the supply of vertical and horizontal controls for the Client’s. Make layout checks on line and grades. Coordination with other Clients. Maintain all file records on designated forms. These records include all reports on work force, equipment, progress, safety, etc. Review and approve the Client’s shop drawings. Locate, execute and interpret all soils and materials testing according to the provisions in the Contract Documents and Specifications. Responsible for the Contract and will not relieve the Clients of his responsibilities without authorization. Responsible for the final materials submittals and approval. Check and certify progress payments to the Clients. Check personnel, equipment and material costs for all Variation Orders and Change Orders required under the terms of the Contract. Review requests for additional compensation and time extension. Prepare Variation Orders for all ‘ Additional ’ and ‘ New’ works. Preparation of the Monthly Report. Site Safety monitoring wherein the monitoring activities, meeting, reviews, safety violations, rectification measures, and accident reports are included in the Monthly Progress Report. When necessary, determine proper costs of additions or credits due to the Client and recommend appropriate action. Inspect safety conditions and inform the Clients of construction safety violations and follow up action to be taken. Qualification Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering Experience Minimum of 20 years experience Experience on similar large scale projects within the Middle East preferred.

jobs.title: Senior Architect
jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: A Senior Site Architect is responsible for overseeing the architectural aspects of construction projects on-site. This role involves ensuring that the design intent is realized during construction, coordinating with various stakeholders, and addressing any issues that arise during the building process. Key Responsibilities: Site Supervision: Oversee the construction site to ensure that the architectural design is implemented correctly and according to the plans. Quality Control: Monitor the quality of materials and workmanship to ensure compliance with design specifications and standards. Coordination: Collaborate with contractors, engineers, and other professionals to resolve any design or construction issues. Compliance: Ensure that all construction activities comply with local building codes, safety regulations, and environmental guidelines. Problem-Solving: Address and resolve any issues or conflicts that arise on-site, making adjustments to plans as necessary. Reporting: Prepare and present progress reports to clients and stakeholders, highlighting any deviations from the plan and proposed solutions. Documentation: Maintain detailed records of site activities, including daily logs, inspection reports, and change orders. Client Interaction: Liaise with clients to understand their needs and ensure their satisfaction with the project. Minimum 15 years demonstrable experience in the all aspects of supervision and project administration of the Architectural works related to large-scale projects within Middle East in the consulting industry. Bachelor Degree in Architectural Engineering or equivalent. Membership of a recognized institution (i.e. PE/Peng, CEng Etc.) would be preferred. LEED GA or AP certifications would be a plus

jobs.title: Graduate - Cost Management Surveyor
jobs.description: Graduates joining the business in a Graduate role and engaging in all elements of the ADVANCE program.  The program is designed to introduce and build essential business and personal skills, helping to enhance the transition from student to professional. Graduates are assigned duties that will provide a broad, well-rounded learning experience within the field they are joining at Graduate entry level. The ADVANCE program lasts approx. 24 months but the successful completion of the program is only to be used as an indicator for strong performance in line with other indicators (strong or above performance rating etc.) We currently have an exciting opportunity for a Quantity Surveying Graduate to join our cost management team in Dubai, UAE. This role offers the chance to provide comprehensive construction cost management services to a diverse portfolio of clients. Key Responsibilities: Assist with pre-contract duties such as cost modelling, cost planning, benchmarking, value and risk analysis, attending design meetings, procurement activities, and preparation of contract documentation. Support post-contract activities including attending site progress meetings, managing valuations, financial reporting, due diligence, and change control procedures. Work autonomously and collaboratively within diverse teams, contributing actively to project delivery. Engage with mentoring and professional development opportunities to support your journey towards chartership. Recently graduated or final year student in Quantity Surveying, Civil Engineering, Construction Engineering or related discipline. Strong motivation to develop technical and professional skills in a multidisciplinary environment. Excellent communication and teamwork abilities. Candidates must be recent graduates, having graduated within the last 24 months or no later than September 2024, to be eligible for AECOM's Graduate Program. Successful applicants must have achieved a minimum GPA of 3.2 and above. If you hold a Master’s Degree, it should have started within 12 months of completing your Bachelor’s Degree to ensure continuous education and meet program requirements.

jobs.title: Technician - Digital Delivery
jobs.description: Job Summary We are seeking an experienced Infrastructure Draftsman with strong expertise in MicroStation, AutoCAD, and Civil 3D to support the design and preparation of detailed drawings for stormwater, sewerage, and microtunneling projects. The ideal candidate will have 7+ years of UAE experience, particularly with RTA and DM-approved projects, and a solid understanding of local authority standards and utility coordination. Key Responsibilities Prepare and revise technical drawings, layouts, and as-built documentation using the latest versions of MicroStation and AutoCAD. Interpret design inputs from engineers and accurately translate them into detailed and coordinated construction drawings. Review, update, and maintain drawings in compliance with project specifications, authority requirements (RTA, DM, DEWA), and AECOM quality standards. Track and verify existing sewerage, stormwater, and irrigation networks in Dubai using as-built records from Drainage (DS), Irrigation (IRR), and Roads projects. Conduct site surveys and coordinate with inspectors to verify construction progress and collect accurate data for as-built documentation. Coordinate closely with engineers, inspectors, and document control teams to ensure timely submission and approval of drawings. Update and prepare redline markups, section details, pipeline profiles, and microtunneling shaft layouts based on field data. Maintain a well-organized archive and digital record system for all project drawings and revisions. Review contractors’ as-built and shop drawing submissions to verify compliance with actual site conditions. Provide technical drafting support during construction to ensure drawings reflect approved changes and actual site execution. Support the preparation of utility coordination drawings, ensuring accuracy across multiple services and avoiding conflicts. Ensure all drawing deliverables meet AECOM and authority CAD standards, including layering, scaling, and title block consistency. Qualifications Diploma or Certificate in Civil Engineering, Drafting, or CAD Design from a recognized institution. Proficient in MicroStation, AutoCAD, and Civil 3D drafting software (latest versions). Knowledge of RTA, DM, DEWA, and other authority drafting standards for infrastructure projects. Familiarity with survey data integration, GIS, and utility coordination is an advantage. Experience Requirements Minimum 7 years of experience in infrastructure drafting, particularly for stormwater, sewerage, and microtunneling projects. Proven experience in preparing as-built drawings, utility layouts, and pipeline profiles. Prior UAE experience, preferably within Dubai, involving RTA and DM-approved projects.

jobs.title: Concept Architect
jobs.description: We are seeking a talented and innovative Concept Architect to join our dynamic design team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative professional with a strong foundation in architectural design to contribute to compelling, multidisciplinary projects. You will work collaboratively with experienced design leadership and cross-functional teams to develop high-quality architectural solutions that balance aesthetic vision with technical precision. This role offers the chance to grow your expertise while working on diverse projects ranging from conceptual design through construction administration. Key responsibilities include: Concept design, optioneering, and design strategy development Computational, generative, and parametric design studies Algorithmic and procedural workflow development Design automation and optimization workflows Environmental and performance-driven analysis using tools such as Ladybug and Galapagos Architectural massing, spatial development, and multidisciplinary coordination AI-enabled design exploration and computational workflow integration Presentation production, visualization direction, and design storytelling Mentoring and supporting junior and graduate architects within the computational design workflow Preferred skills and software proficiency: Rhino Grasshopper Revit Adobe Creative Suite Python scripting and AI-integrated workflows Strong understanding of computational design methodologies, procedural workflows, and logical problem-solving approaches Rendering and visualization story telling      Bachelor's Degree in Architecture, Architectural Design, or related field (Master's Degree preferred) Minimum 5+ years of professional experience in architectural design or demonstrated equivalent experience

jobs.title: Material Inspector
jobs.description: Job Summary AECOM is seeking an experienced Materials Inspector for a major Roads and Infrastructure, NDRC, Stormwater, sewerange lines projects located in Dubai. The role involves both inspection and the supervision of Roads and Infrastructure works of the project and to ensure that the activities are being executed in accordance with the project specification. • Inspect site conditions prior to the start of construction activities to verify the compatibility of the site with the approved design and permanent works. • Check for obstructions and document existing site conditions through photographs to prevent potential damage to existing utilities, structures, and landscaping. • Review and verify materials delivered to the site for conformance with approved samples, specifications, and equipment lists; report any non-conformances to the Resident Engineer (RE). • Ensure that all materials are properly stored and protected at the site to maintain quality and prevent damage or contamination. • Inspect construction of both temporary and permanent works to confirm compliance with contract documents, approved shop drawings, and project specifications. • Prepare and maintain accurate daily inspection logs, test reports, and site records in accordance with the Contract Administration Procedures Manual. • Monitor site safety practices and ensure that all works are carried out following approved safety procedures; report unsafe practices or incidents to the RE immediately. • Issue Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) and Site Observation Reports (SORs) for any deviation from approved materials, specifications, or procedures, and follow up on corrective actions. • Assist in the inspection of completed works, prepare punch lists, and monitor testing, commissioning, and handover activities to ensure quality compliance. • Review and provide recommendations on Contractors’ Requests for Information (RFIs) related to civil materials and testing requirements. • Evaluate requests for material variances or substitutions and provide technical comments and recommendations to the RE for approval. • Read and interpret working drawings and related specifications, enforce compliance on-site, and prepare inspection and material testing reports. • Coordinate with third-party testing laboratories and ensure all material tests are performed as per project specifications and standards. • Ensure that all material approvals, test results, NCRs, and inspection records are properly documented and maintained for monthly reporting and project close-out. • Coordinate with relevant authorities for material approvals, testing, and compliance verification.   Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Relevant Technical Field 5 + years of relevant experience in laboratory materials testing including field inspection of samples and materials. Middle East experience preferred DCL  approval is preferred Relevant experience in comparable roads, NDRC, Stormwater, sewerage lines, pump station, irrigation works etc.

jobs.location: Abu Dhabi, UAE, United Arab Emirates
jobs.description: • Manages documents throughout all phases of a project. • Maintains project directory. • Maintains and files reproduced document originals, as well as revisions for easy retrieval. • Develops project-specific filing structure and document control procedures, including implementing into required electronic document management systems (EDMS). • Records and stores photographs. • Under general supervision, performs general duties that are often routine and repetitive. • Under general supervision, interacts with professional/technical staff to accomplish assigned objectives.  • Requires limited exercise of judgment. • Receives guidance on new aspects of assignments. As part of AECOM’s commitment to Emiratisation and in accordance with UAE labour law, only UAE Nationals with a family book will be considered for this role.

jobs.title: Senior Engineer - Planning
jobs.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Engineer - Planning to join our team in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. In this role, you will be responsible for planning and overseeing complex infrastructure projects, ensuring efficient and sustainable urban development in one of the world's most dynamic cities. Develop comprehensive infrastructure plans for urban development projects, including transportation networks, utilities, and public facilities Conduct feasibility studies and cost-benefit analyses for proposed infrastructure projects Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including architects, environmental specialists, and government officials Create and maintain project schedules, budgets, and resource allocation plans Perform site assessments and analyze data to inform infrastructure planning decisions Ensure compliance with local building codes, regulations, and environmental standards Prepare and present technical reports, proposals, and presentations to stakeholders Implement sustainable and innovative solutions in infrastructure design and planning Monitor project progress and make necessary adjustments to meet deadlines and quality standards Mentor junior engineers and contribute to the overall growth of the engineering team Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Urban Planning, or a related field Minimum of 12 years of experience in infrastructure planning and engineering Professional Engineer (PE) certification preferred; Project Management Professional (PMP) certification is a plus Proficiency in project management software (e.g., Microsoft Project, Primavera P6) Strong skills in AutoCAD or similar CAD software and GIS applications Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel, PowerPoint, and Word Demonstrated experience with urban development projects and infrastructure systems In-depth knowledge of local building codes and regulations in the UAE Familiarity with environmental impact assessments and sustainable urban development practices Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a keen attention to detail Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams Experience working in the Middle East, particularly in the UAE, is highly desirable

jobs.title: Forward Deployment Engineer
jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.
Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan.

We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster.

The Mission
We believe the transformative potential of AI is significantly under-realised in our region. To bridge this gap, we are incubating a new division within Careem dedicated to Enterprise AI Transformation.
We are reimagining and building the future of work to simplify and improve how businesses function in the era of GenAI with WorkOS: a next-generation ‘operating system’ designed to deploy, orchestrate, and improve AI agents across every customer and operational journey (and increasingly autonomously). This isn’t just automation; it’s about creating a hybrid workforce where self-learning AI agents handle the heavy lifting, empowering our people to focus on the highest-value, most creative work.

The Team
This group operates as a startup within Careem with a distinct mandate: move fast, build boldly, and deliver production-grade AI systems that fundamentally change how the region leverages technology.

The Opportunity
We are looking for an exceptional founding Backend Engineer to build the WorkOS platform. This is a role for a systems-thinker who wants to work at the intersection of high-scale distributed systems and AI orchestration. You will be responsible for building the "brain" of WorkOS, the engine that deploys and manages autonomous agents across the region’s most complex operational journeys at massive scale.

As one of the first engineers in WorkOS, you will have a disproportionate impact on our technical foundation, culture, and the speed at which we move from 0 to 1.

What You’ll Do

  • Architect and Build the WorkOS Engine: Design and build the scalable backend services and architectures required to orchestrate complex AI workflows and real-time data streaming. Leverage novel technologies to build cutting-edge capabilities.
  • Set the Engineering Gold Standard: As a founding member, you will establish our backend best practices, from CI/CD and observability to security and system integrity, ensuring a high-velocity but rigorous engineering culture.
  • Solve for Distributed Complexity: Navigate the unique challenges of integrating AI agents into legacy enterprise stacks, ensuring low-latency communication and robust data consistency.
  • Database design: Build our data infrastructure, leveraging relational, schemaless, and vect …

jobs.description:
Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires.

The mission

We are reimagining and building the future of work to simplify and improve how businesses function in the era of GenAI with WorkOS: a next-generation ‘operating system’ designed to deploy, orchestrate, and improve AI agents across every customer and operational journey (and increasingly autonomously). This isn’t just automation; it’s about creating a hybrid workforce where self-learning AI agents handle the heavy lifting, empowering our people to focus on the highest-value, most creative work.

The team
This group operates as a startup within Careem with a distinct mandate: move fast, build boldly, and deliver production-grade AI systems that fundamentally change how the region leverages technology.

The opportunity

We are looking for a Founding Forward Deployed Engineer - WorkOS - Enterprise Agentic AI to be the vanguard of the WorkOS mission. This is a role for a "heretic": someone who cares more about solving the problem and delivering value than following a rigid spec. You will be the bridge between our core AI engine and the messy, high-stakes reality of our customers’ operations.
As an FDE, you aren't sitting in the back office; you are on the front lines, embedded with internal Careem verticals and external enterprise partners. In partnership with your implementation squad, consisting also of business transformation consultants, you will identify their deepest friction points and build the "special ops" code that proves the power of WorkOS. Your work is the proof of concept that will redefine how the region works.

What You’ll Do
  • Tip of the Spear: Lead the deployment of WorkOS in the field. You will be responsible for understanding a customer's operational "chaos" and architecting an AI-driven solution that fixes it in weeks, not months.
  • Rapid-Fire Execution: Build, iterate, and ship. You will write high-quality, full-stack code to create bespoke integrations and augment our platform’s core capabilities and create custom agents. This goes beyond context engineering; you’ll build the connective tissue - integrating bespoke APIs, setting up RAGs and Evals, tuning the E2E system and performance, and architecting multi-agent handoffs.
  • Feedback Loop Mastery: You are the eyes and ears of the product team. Y …

jobs.title: Binance Accelerator Program - Content Specialist
jobs.description: Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world. About Binance Accelerator Program Binance Accelerator Program is a concise fixed-term program designed for Early Career Talent to have an immersive experience in the rapidly expanding Web3 space. jobs.title: Director, Business Development, Corporate Solutions - B2B Acceptance
jobs.description: Our Purpose Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. Title and Summary Director, Business Development, Corporate Solutions - B2B Acceptance Overview This role sits within Corporate Solutions and is responsible for developing and scaling Mastercard’s B2B Acceptance strategy across East + West Arabia and Eastern Europe. You will help acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and merchants unlock new commercial payment flows. B2B Acceptance is a significant growth opportunity. Corporate buyers increasingly want to pay suppliers using commercial and virtual cards, particularly for invoice-based payments. For acquirers and merchants, this creates an opportunity to capture new high-value flows, improve cash flow, reduce manual processes and enable better reconciliation through data and straight-through processing. Key questions • Do you have a track record of building and scaling commercial payment products or acceptance-led growth strategies? • Can you influence senior executives across banks, acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and large merchants? • Are you motivated by solving commercial and operational problems through technology, data and partnerships? • Can you turn market trends into execution plans that drive GDV, revenue and merchant activation? Role • Develop and execute the EEMEA B2B Acceptance strategy to retain, grow and expand Mastercard’s commercial acceptance footprint. • Drive incremental commercial volume and revenue by enabling acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and merchants to capture high-value B2B and invoice-based payment flows. • Remove operational barriers to B2B acceptance, including onboarding friction, manual processing, reconciliation challenges, supplier education and acceptance visibility. • Build and scale market strategies for virtual card acceptance, supplier enablement, straight-through processing and automated reconciliation. • Provide thought leadership to global product teams on B2B merchant, acquiring, fintech, PSP, AP/AR, ERP and embedded finance opportunities. • Lead digital presence, market insights, research and sales enablement for Commercial Acceptance across Eastern Europe and the Middle East. • Build educational collateral and go-to-market playbooks to support banks, acquirers, fintechs, merchants and internal teams. • Partner with Commercial Solutions, issuers, acquirers, PSPs, fintechs and account teams to activate commercial acceptance assets and partnerships. • Manage dashboards and KPIs covering merchant activation, supplier enablement, GDV, net revenue, pipeline conversion and partner performance. • Ensure Mastercard resources are deployed effectively to support commercial outcomes, market priorities and scalable regional growth. All About You • Strong understanding of acquiring, merchant economics, commercial card acceptance, virtual cards and B2B payment flows. • Proven ability to build and scale product value propositions, particularly across acquiring, merchant or supplier enablement. • Experience in Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, ERP, procurement, embedded finance or invoice-based payments is a strong advantage. • Strong commercial mindset, with experience managing revenue growth, pipeline execution, business performance or P&L-related outcomes. • Executive presence and consultative sales experience, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders internally and externally. • Strong analytical capability, including the ability to use data to build business cases, track performance and shape strategy. • Collaborative, entrepreneurial and execution-f …

jobs.title: Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor
jobs.location: AE-ABU DHABI-AL GHAITH YARD, MUSSAFAH INDUSTRIAL 16th STREET
jobs.description: Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor Are you a Mechanical Field Service Engineer looking for an interesting and inspiring opportunity? Would you like to be part of our Turbomachinery Process Solutions Product Line? Join our Industrial and Energy Technology Team! Our Industrial and Energy Technology (IET) operates across 120 countries and serves the entire energy value chain and industrial sector. We hold an extensive portfolio of rotating equipment, process flow, transmission technology and lifecycle services. Our Engineering Transformation teamwork to improve the quality of our Engineering processes. Partner with the best As a Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressors, you will be coordinating and overseeing work at the field site, providing quality service to the customer. In addition, your Job role may include providing the planning necessary for the job including instructions to the crew and equipment. As a Field Service Engineer - Centrifugal Compressor, you will be responsible for Providing technical advice and support to clients and craft-labour in the installation, commissioning and maintenance of Baker Hughes-supplied Centrifugal Compressors. Unloading and Inspection of the Centrifugal Compressor & its major auxiliaries at the installation site and their placement on the foundation. Improving Geometric dimensional check of foundation, equipment and auxiliary skids positioning, levelling, and alignment Performing Installation, commissioning & Startup of various types of Centrifugal Compressors MCL, BCL, PCL, and SRL supplied by Baker Hughes at customer sites. Performing minor & major inspections of Centrifugal Compressors. Involving assembly, disassembly, checking and adjusting clearances evaluating wear and damage state of Couplings, lubricated-tooth, and dry-elastic types, thrust bearings and journal bearings of various kinds, gas seals, labyrinth and honeycomb types, Oil seals, gauged floating rings types, Dry gas seals or oil mechanical seals, Labyrinth or honeycomb inner seals, Diaphragms or diaphragms bundle and volutes. Fuel your passion To be successful in this role you will: Have a bachelor’s in engineering with 5+ years of relevant industry experience. Have working experience in Rotating equipment in the Field at Customer Sites. Have excellent Functional/Technical, Customer Focus, Communication, Interpersonal, and Team working skills. Have prior experience in Installation and/or Maintenance activities of Centrifugal Compressors in the Field at Customer Sites Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. In this role, we can offer the following flexible working patterns: When assignments are complete, in general, you won't be required to visit the office, however, you can operate from Home and perform basic tasks like completing previous assignment reports, mobilization-related aspects, etc before you take on your next projects. The company shall ensure a work-life balance for the individual. Working with us Our people are at the heart of what we do at Baker Hughes.
